scored_tweets.text: At @11x_official, we designed Julian to help customers capture inbound demand through an always-on digital SDR that qualifies prospects by calling them seconds after they fill out a form. Now we're making Julian smarter - he can warm up prospects via SMS and WhatsApp before giving them a call. He can follow up after a call for meeting confirmations, reminders, or even send more information.

.@11x_official shares their experience rebuilding their Alice Agent using our full stack for building agents -- LangGraph, LangGraph Platform, LangSmith, and LangChain. How do you build AI voice agents that sound real? I spoke with @flanciscoz (@11x_official) about latency, empathy, turn-taking, and why perfection kills authenticity in voice AI. Sub-300ms latency is just the start. Here's what stood out 🧵 https://t.co/LQZuZ7GUVE

Right after hitting $10m cARR, @11x_official rebuilt their core product, Alice, from scratch to be an agent! @shcallaway and Keith Fearon share why and how: - Models and frameworks (LangGraph) improved, @replit agent blew them away and convinced then agents are here - Use a vanilla tech stack, lean on vendors, innovate elsewhere. LangGraph Platform was a natural choice! - For campaign creation, began with a simple ReAct architecture, added more structure (workflows) for reliability, then went multiagent for flexibility! But simplicity is best in a vacuum - Tools are more useful to an agent than innate prior knowledge

This simple chart captures one of my biggest learnings from the past 9 months of building AI agents at @11x_official. To explain: adding structure to your agent can provide performance gains in the short-term, but this structure can actually make your agent perform _worse_ in the long-term (as the underlying models get better). Credit to @swyx for surfacing the chart in his latest blog post, "In the Matter of OpenAI vs LangGraph", and for re-framing the chart in terms of agent performance (as opposed to model performance). Credit to @hwchung27 for the original!

Creative Your Burnout Morning Poster (Customized to Your Personality & Aesthetic) Created using @AdobeFirefly Partnered Model Prompt - Use my uploaded face as the ONLY facial reference. Preserve my exact identity, facial structure, expressive eyes, skin tone, lips, nose shape, and recognizable features with zero facial drift. Create an ultra-detailed cinematic 3D chibi-style portrait inspired by premium Pixar and modern collectible character art. The character should represent an exhausted but genius-level AI creator girl after an all-night creative session. Scene: She is sitting sleepily on the edge of a rustic wooden table early in the morning, looking emotionally drained but unintentionally adorable. Messy fluffy hair tied loosely in a chaotic bun with strands falling everywhere, sleepy half-open eyes, soft pout, slightly red nose, tired “I edited all night” expression. She wears oversized pastel sleepwear with subtle creative-tech aesthetics: soft ivory pajama set tiny “Future Vibes AI” embroidery cozy cardigan slipping off one shoulder fluffy duck slippers delicate pearl earrings Environment: Warm moody morning atmosphere with soft cinematic lighting, steam rising from a cup of coffee beside her, blurred artistic studio background, creative workspace energy. Around the character include subtle hints of her personality: tiny floating holographic video timelines miniature film clapperboard AI art sketches tiny glowing travel icons scattered sticky notes with creative ideas small laptop showing unfinished edits scribbled concepts YouTube play icon soft pastel doodles Expression & Mood: She should look like: an overthinking creative genius emotionally exhausted but lovable sleep-deprived filmmaker energy “just one more edit” vibe quiet chaos mixed with soft humor Style: Ultra-premium stylized realism, collectible figurine quality, ultra-detailed hair strands, realistic fabric texture, soft skin shading, cinematic depth of field, dreamy muted pastel tones, cozy Pinterest aesthetic, subtle humor, emotional storytelling. Lighting: Soft window light, warm cinematic shadows, gentle volumetric haze, realistic steam glow from coffee. Color Palette: Mocha brown, warm cream, dusty peach, muted teal, ivory, soft caramel, pastel gold accents. Composition: Centered symmetrical composition, shallow depth of field, highly expressive face, clean luxury aesthetic, social-media-ready, 8K ultra detailed. Add tiny handwritten doodles around the scene: “rendering…” “creative chaos” “future vibes” “1% battery” “one more export” Negative Prompt: bad anatomy, distorted face, extra fingers, plastic skin, overexposed highlights, blurry eyes, cartoonish proportions, low detail, bad hands, uncanny face, oversaturated colors, duplicate objects, warped furniture, messy composition, low resolution.

Thank you so much to everyone who came out to the Low Latency Club meetup at @telnyx last night and shared what they're building in voice AI! Big thanks to Telnyx and @AssemblyAI for hosting and co-sponsoring, and a huge thank you to all the speakers and attendees who made the night so valuable. A big shoutout to our CEO @RohanVasishth , Anusha Thruval, and @ryanseams for sharing their insights throughout the night. A few themes that came up throughout the night: • Real-world audio conditions (background noise, AirPods on a busy street) are where speech models are truly tested, and closing that gap is still one of the hardest problems in the space. • Latency is a full-stack challenge. Carrier hops, inference speed, and context window size all add up, and owning your infrastructure makes a real difference. • Inconsistent quality in voice agents usually comes down to orchestration and configuration, not just the model itself. • Giving voice agents dynamic context in real time remains one of the biggest unsolved problems, especially in high-stakes conversations. See you at the next one. 👋

This is the future of coding agents: specialized, high-speed subagents handling critical workflows in the background. Proud to see @augmentcode running Mercury 2 in production for context compaction with major latency and cost improvements.

@augmentcode rebuilt their context compaction layer around Mercury 2. 82% latency cut. 90% cost cut. Comparable quality to Opus 4.7. Running in production today. "We took a counter-intuitive bet. We decoupled summarization entirely, offloading it to Mercury 2 as a dedicated subagent. Mercury 2 is the highly efficient engine powering our most critical workflows." -@RustagiAnkur & @jm1234567890, Members of Technical Staff at Augment Code The subagent layer needs the most efficient model. Full methodology and eval setup in the writeup. https://t.co/LPVTdaMjli

Congrats to the Webwright team https://t.co/mmpl4tO0p4 at @MSFTResearch for taking the #1 spot on Odysseys, a highly challenging benchmark for long-horizon web agents: https://t.co/rj5BHK5g6C Odysseys evaluates realistic, multi-hour web workflows that require sustained planning, memory, reasoning, and verification across many websites and tools. These are far beyond short single-step browser tasks. For example, if you are searching for CS faculty positions, a single task could involve building a comprehensive Excel tracker of openings across the top CS schools using CSRankings as the master checklist; verifying every school directly through department, engineering, and university careers pages for CS/AI/ML/data science/robotics/vision faculty roles; opening and validating each posting; maintaining structured evidence and verification tabs; and finishing with a completeness audit and summary of hiring trends. Exciting progress toward truly capable long-horizon web agents.

2026 marks a decade since I moved to the U.S. for school. After 10 years of learning, navigating academia, and finding my communities along the way, I am SO excited to share that I've received my Ph.D. from @cmuhcii, @CarnegieMellon and joined @MSFTResearch (MSR), New York City as a Senior Researcher!!! At MSR, I am thrilled to be able to work at the intersection of research, product, and policy within the 𝐅𝐀𝐓𝐄 (𝐅𝐚𝐢𝐫𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬, 𝐀𝐜𝐜𝐨𝐮𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐛𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲, 𝐓𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐩𝐚𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐲, 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐄𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐜𝐬 𝐢𝐧 𝐀𝐈) 𝐠𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐩 and closely with the 𝐒𝐓𝐀𝐂 (𝐒𝐨𝐜𝐢𝐨-𝐓𝐞𝐜𝐡𝐧𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐥 𝐀𝐥𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐂𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐫) 𝐭𝐞𝐚𝐦 — partnering with product teams across Microsoft to translate rigorous research into practice. My goal for the next stage of my career is to 𝐨𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐳𝐞 𝐀𝐈 𝐞𝐯𝐚𝐥𝐮𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐦𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐜𝐡 𝐢𝐧 𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐥-𝐰𝐨𝐫𝐥𝐝 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐭 𝐝𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐥𝐨𝐩𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 to ensure safer and more responsible AI, with a particular focus on building tools and processes to evaluate agentic AI systems and supporting meaningful human engagement in human–agent collaboration. I am tremendously grateful to everyone who's been part of this journey, and excited for what's ahead!!

Nice drop from @philipkiely and @baseten. 📗 Inference Engineering maps the stack behind modern AI inference — runtimes, infrastructure, and tooling — and digs into the practical details of serving LLMs on NVIDIA GPUs with TensorRT LLM and Dynamo. ICYMI — worth the read. 👇

Most AI teams are flying blind on inference performance. They ship, they scale, something breaks and they don't know why. Since joining @baseten the most common question I get is - how are you winning in a space moving this fast? 😅 So I’m starting a small thing - a few posts every week on the HOW behind what we ship: docs, launches, case studies, performance work. More like a running notebook. I’ll mostly point to what our team publishes and add my own notes. Let's call it Inference Drops? Inference Drops #1 — Benchmarks My take on benchmarks: they’re only useful if you can reproduce them on your own workload. Otherwise you end up optimizing for someone else’s chart… and it shows up later in p99. Our engineering team published a step-by-step on reproducing @SemiAnalysis_ InferenceMAX (cool work) on a dedicated deployment (4×B200s). We set up the config, ran the benchmark scripts. Next question comes, client-side vs server-side benchmarking? Server-side tells you what the hardware stack can do. Client-side tells you what users actually experience. Since InferenceMAX benchmarks and compares hardware performance, server-side was the right approach here. (attached results) I agree with the underlying point: you should start benchmarking earlier than feels necessary. It becomes your early warning system as you scale. What are you stuck on right now when it comes to benchmarking? Part 2 is coming soon (latency-sensitive workloads like codegen). Blog link in next post..

When was the last time you called your customer and asked him - Hey, can I do something for you? We know that's a hard task if your business has thousands of customers. But someone has figured out an elegant way. @usebland is the first tool that allows anyone to build Production-Ready Voice Agents from a single Prompt. What the hell does that mean? Norm, which is a part of the Bland interface, lets you automate thousands, even millions of phone calls with a single prompt. Bland is fully self-hosted, meaning your data stays entirely secure from end-to-end. And it has the lowest latency on the planet, making your calls sound human. Each customer gets their own dedicated instance, ensuring maximum security, reliability, and control. You can deploy your instance within the Bland infrastructure, on-premise, or in your VPC. We need to repeat this because it is insanely cool: one prompt - millions of phone calls. Know more on https://t.co/SSaCobMn7K and tune in to their live show at 1:30 pm EST. Disclaimer: This content was produced in collaboration with the client and is intended for informational purposes only. It does not constitute financial or investment advice. Always conduct your own research before making any financial decisions.

AI-generated software is becoming its own software supply chain. @cursor_ai, @Lovable, @Replit, @boltdotnew,@claudeai, @grok, agents, dApps, MCP tools, all of them are moving code from prompt to production faster than traditional security workflows can keep up. That creates a new requirement: security validation has to become model-native. Nullsec-S1 is being built for that layer. A security-specialized LLM that sits between AI-generated code and production, analyzing auth logic, secrets, env exposure, SQLi, SSRF, XSS, MCP/tool abuse, shell execution, dependency risk, wallet flows, and safe-code calibration. AI builds the software. Nullsec-S1 validates it.

Everyone is talking about AI. I've been having a lot more fun vibe coding with these 5 underrated tools lately: 1. @cline Open-source agent that can edit files, use the terminal and browse the web directly from VS Code 2. @opencode Runs in the terminal and can work across your entire codebase using affordable models 3. @AskAider AI pair programmer with Git integration, making code changes and commits simple 4. @continuedev Open-source coding assistant that lets you use almost any AI model with full control @RooCode Multi-agent assistant with dedicated modes for coding, architecture, debugging and more If you're still only using ChatGPT or Cursor, these are worth checking out. Which one would you try first?

. @aiDotEngineer I talk about how @cline we're on a mission to fight slop in coding agents. Slop is the worst part of building in this space. Scroll any timeline and you'll see people flexing about merging 100 commits a day. It creates a subconscious pressure to optimize for throughput with more agents and more commits. But take a step back: is that actually the right goal? Do we really believe more code and more agents is inherently better? What if we slowed down and sharpened the axe instead, investing in better baseline scaffolding, and building simpler but far more powerful agents that can handle millions of tasks seamlessly at scale? I dive deeper into this in my talk would love for you to give it a listen.

Cline SDK 终于发布了，同时发布的还有以 Cline SDK 为基础的 Cline CLI 和 Skills @cline 是最早一批 Agentic Coding 工具了，之前主要以 IDE 插件的形式存在，Cline 的实际表现一直都很强，而且团队有很多技术工程实践，都早于 Claude Code 等团队，技术博客价值很高！后来经历了一些变动，工程团队很多人都去了 Codex，现在重新看到 Cline 的最新进展，还是很期待实际表现。 https://t.co/Wh1DuFewHI 最新发布的 Cline CLI 在 Terminal Benchmark 上多项第一，超过 Claude Code、Codex 和 Droid 等 Agent，咱们一起看看它在 Harness 方面的重要实践。 Cline 在 Harness 方面的升级 Cline 2.0 重写了 prompts、简化 loop、收紧上下文管理、改进反馈与错误处理、重新设计了工具如何暴露给模型。官方公布的 Terminal-Bench 2.0 成绩： 1. 前沿模型上 Cline CLI 的表现： · claude-opus-4.7：74.2%（Claude Code 同模型 69.4%） · claude-opus-4.6：71.9%（Claude Code 65.4%，Droid 69.9%） · gpt-5.3-codex：73.0%（Codex CLI 75.1%、Droid 77.3% 略高） 2. 开源权重模型上更明显的领先： · kimi-k2.6：Cline 55.1% vs OpenCode 37.1% / Pi-Code 45.5% · deepseek-v4-pro：53.9% vs 51.7% / 52.9% 值得关注的能力点 1. Plugin 层：可注册工具、监听生命周期、加规则与命令、塑造 agent 视野。可从单个 .ts 文件起步，逐步演进为带 cline.plugins manifest 的包。 2. Provider 开放性：@ cline/llms 把模型目录与 provider 配置从 agent loop 中剥离，支持 Anthropic / OpenAI / Google / Bedrock / Mistral / LiteLLM / vLLM / Together / Fireworks。新 provider 只需实现 ApiHandler 并 registerHandler。 3. 原生 Agent Teams / Subagents：子 agent 有自己的模型、工具、prompt，bundled plugin 提供启动、消息、状态、handoff notes 等工具——不需要自己写 orchestration。 4. 开箱即用：CRON、checkpointing、Web search、MCP connector 全部原生。 5. CLI Connectors（实验性）：cline connect 向导可把 agent 接入 Telegram / WhatsApp / Slack。

Lessons from years of building, breaking and rewriting coding agents have been condensed into this new @cline SDK harness. There's a point in software engineering when incremental changes just don't cut it anymore and you are tempted to rewrite from scratch. You are almost always better off not giving in to that temptation. Except, coding agent codebases are small enough that it makes perfect sense to rework the core harness logic from scratch. Why exactly did we do this though? - We spent an ungodly amount of money on evals to squeeze the alpha of frontier models and we learnt that the new models are much simpler to use. This means: simpler system prompt, and fewer tools. We had to get out of the way of the model doing its magic. - There was just too much fluff and un-needed logic to handle the edge cases for previous models that wasn't needed anymore and it lead to a lot of dead spaghetti code - We wanted to make something really extensible that just about anyone can use to plug and play. Most good SDKs these days are closed source and you don't really know how they work, with the Cline opensource SDK people can really dive in how they can make incredible tooling on top of an agentic harness and we too get to learn from our incredible opensource community. This project is a labour of love and so many incredible ideas have been condensed into this. We have shown so much restraint both with what we added and especially with what we didn't add here. Please try this out and let us know how it feels. All you have to do is: npm install @cline/sdk

App-layer tech moats are truly gone🌬️ This weekend I joined @agihouse_org for an AI x Fintech hackathon. 150+ builders crafted credit underwriting tools, onchain CLOs, agentic commerce apps, and more in 1-2 hours. (h/t @codeiumdev for speed boosts) I also hopped on a panel with Visa Ventures + @generalcatalyst + @FinVentureCap + @getfinvest. Takeaways - Technical moats are now ~zero. See above. You can't appreciate the power until you use them or see a power-user apply them up close - Founders are in a challenging spot – one of your key moats (technical prowess + shipping speed) faded overnight. Investors are indexing much more heavily on unique GTM, data advantages, some combo of specialization + taste. even at seed - Venture & growth are now large enough where finding the right fund to pitch is as important as your pitch. It’s not one size fits all - all 4 funds on the panel represented different risk appetites, check sizes, and deployment pacing - Lastly, it was incredible to meet devs who uprooted their lives from Poland, Argentina, Czechia, China, and beyond to join the 'AI moment' in SF. No plan, no network, living in garages… The Bay is still the global beacon for tech innovation and their optimism was refreshing Thanks @naomiiixia for hosting! @AGIHouseSF

𝐖𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐢𝐬 Codeium 𝐀𝐛𝐨𝐮𝐭? @codeiumdev is an AI-powered platform designed to accelerate software development. It’s essentially a toolkit that helps developers write code faster and more efficiently by leveraging advanced artificial intelligence. The company aims to eliminate the tedious, repetitive parts of coding, like writing boilerplate code or digging through documentation, allowing developers to focus on creativity and problem-solving. Their offerings include tools for code autocompletion, intelligent code search, and an AI-driven chat feature, all integrated into various development environments. 𝐖𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐀𝐫𝐞 𝐓𝐡𝐞𝐲 𝐁𝐮𝐢𝐥𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠? Codeium is building a suite of AI-driven tools to enhance the coding workflow. Key products include: 1 . Code Autocomplete: Suggests code snippets in real-time as developers type, supporting over 70 programming languages and integrating with more than 40 integrated development environments (IDEs) like Visual Studio Code, JetBrains, and Jupyter Notebooks. 2 . AI Chat: An in-editor assistant that lets developers ask questions, refactor code, generate boilerplate, or get explanations in natural language. 3. Code Search: Allows developers to search their codebase using natural language queries, making it easier to navigate complex projects. 4. Windsurf Editor: A newer addition, described as an "agentic IDE" (launched in November 2024 per their X posts), which combines deep codebase understanding with real-time awareness of a developer’s actions to provide proactive, context-aware assistance. Their focus is on creating a seamless, productivity-boosting experience for developers, with an emphasis on enterprise-grade security and flexibility (e.g., self-hosted options for companies). 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐞𝐭𝐢𝐭𝐨𝐫𝐬 Codeium operates in the growing market of AI-powered coding assistants. Its main competitors include: 1 . GitHub Copilot: Developed by GitHub and OpenAI, this is the market leader, offering similar code completion and chat features. It’s deeply integrated with GitHub’s ecosystem and powered by models like Codex and GPT-4. 2. Tabnine: Another AI coding assistant that provides code completions and supports multiple IDEs, with a focus on privacy and customizable models. 3. Cursor: An AI-first code editor that competes with Codeium’s Windsurf Editor, offering pair-programming-like features with AI. 4. Amazon CodeWhisperer: A machine learning-powered tool from AWS, aimed at speeding up development with context-aware suggestions, especially for AWS-related projects. 5. Sourcegraph’s Cody: An AI coding assistant focused on codebase understanding and team consistency, competing with Codeium’s search and enterprise features. Other players like JetBrains AI, https://t.co/kVUT93qHsl, and smaller startups (e.g., MutableAI, Supermaven) also vie for a share of this space, but the above are the most direct rivals. 𝐀𝐝𝐯𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐠𝐞𝐬 1 . Free Tier: Codeium offers a robust free plan with unlimited autocomplete and chat features, making it accessible to individual developers, unlike Copilot’s paid-only model. 2. Wide IDE Support: It integrates with over 40 IDEs, including niche ones like Jupyter and Colab, giving it broader compatibility than some competitors. 3. Privacy and Security: Codeium emphasizes that it doesn’t train its models on private user data, and it offers self-hosted deployment options for enterprises, appealing to security-conscious organizations. 4. Customizability: Enterprises can fine-tune models with their own codebases, and the Windsurf Editor promises advanced, proactive AI assistance. 5. Cost-Effectiveness: Paid plans (e.g., $15/month for Pro, custom pricing for enterprises) are competitive, especially for teams compared to Copilot’s pricing. 𝐃𝐢𝐬𝐚𝐝𝐯𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐠𝐞𝐬 1. Model Accuracy: While Codeium uses proprietary models (and optionally GPT-4 in premium plans), some users report that competitors like Copilot might edge out in suggestion accuracy or speed due to their reliance on cutting-edge OpenAI models. 2. Documentation Gaps: The platform’s documentation is sometimes criticized for being sparse, which can make onboarding or troubleshooting harder compared to well-documented rivals like Copilot. 3. Maturity: As a newer player (founded in 2021, pivoted in 2022), Codeium may lack the ecosystem depth and user base of Copilot, which benefits from GitHub’s massive community. 4. Occasional Bugs: Users have noted quirks, like the VS Code extension occasionally requiring restarts, suggesting it’s still refining its polish. 5. Market Perception: With Copilot dominating mindshare, Codeium has to work harder to prove its differentiation, especially as AI coding tools become commoditized. 𝙁𝙞𝙣𝙖𝙡 𝙏𝙝𝙤𝙪𝙜𝙝𝙩𝙨 Codeium is carving out a niche by offering a free, privacy-focused, and highly compatible AI coding assistant, with ambitious projects like the Windsurf Editor aiming to redefine how developers interact with AI. It competes head-on with giants like GitHub Copilot and smaller innovators like Tabnine, balancing affordability and flexibility against the challenges of matching the polish and scale of more established tools. For developers or teams prioritizing cost, security, and broad IDE support, it’s a strong contender, though it’s still proving itself against the heavyweights.

Running Qwen Image locally on my @AMD RX 7800 XT via @ComfyUI + ROCm. Setup: >GPU: AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT, 16GB VRAM >Backend: PyTorch ROCm >UI: ComfyUI >Model loader: ComfyUI-GGUF >Diffusion model: qwen-image-2512-Q4_K_M.gguf >Text encoder: qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled.safetensors >VAE: qwen_image_vae.safetensors The GGUF diffusion model loads onto the GPU through UnetLoaderGGUF, with the Qwen text encoder handling prompt conditioning and the VAE decoding the final latent into an image. Local @AMD image gen is getting pretty fun.

$Hirys fam, meet @crawl4ai, AI’s librarian for the jungle of the web. You ever try to research something and find 20 tabs open, half of them useless noise? Crawl4AI turns that chaos into clean, usable pieces. It crawls pages, filters what matters, then spits everything out in Markdown or structured JSON, ready for agents, LLMs, or dashboards. Here’s where it gets juicy: with @irys_xyz, every crawl becomes a timestamped story. Not just what was scraped, but why and when. If a scraper pulls data for a trend report, months later someone can check the exact snapshot, the schema, even see errors that were ignored. No more “data exists but can’t prove if it’s real.” Crawl4AI gets you the inputs. Irys gives you verifiable history. This isn’t just about extracting. It’s about holding the evidence.

this is the most underrated update in the agent space right now. your AI workflow runs for 47 minutes, burns 312 LLM calls, then crashes at step 8. most frameworks make you restart from zero. @crewAIInc just shipped checkpointing. think google docs autosave, but for your agent's work-in-progress. every flow method becomes a recovery point. resume in one line. fork from any saved state into a new branch. edit past outputs and watch changes ripple downstream. visual TUI to inspect everything. your pipelines stop being fragile one-shot jobs. they become resumable, inspectable, branchable processes. zero extra infra. 100% open-source. get started with CrewAI here: https://t.co/bPGE0cGOIm (don't forget to star it ⭐️)

ampersend is now integrated with @crewAIInc 🚀 give your crewai agents a smart wallet and spend controls in 35 lines of python: 1️⃣ the agent hits an x402 endpoint 2️⃣ ampersend intercepts the 402 3️⃣ ampersend signs the payment with the agent's session key 4️⃣ settles in $usdc on @base, and retries automatically. authentication, billing infrastructure, and api overhead are fully abstracted away. point it at any x402-enabled service and your agents are transacting onchain with guardrails built in from the start. repo & step-by-step guide 👇

Now, with fresh capital, the company is set to accelerate U.S. expansion and aim for $100M ARR. Well done to @tal_kollender , @raz_gilad, Yaakov Kogan, and the entire Remedio team for proving bootstrapped success can go global. Funding was led by @BessemerVP, with participation from @TLV_Partners and Picture Capital. 3. NextVision To Raise $400M Amid Global Demand For Drone Tech NextVision Stabilized Systems (TASE: NXSN), a leader in stabilized cameras for drones and defense vehicles, is set to secure a $400M raise as it eyes entry into the Tel Aviv 35 Index. With soaring share growth and rising defense budgets worldwide, the company is positioned at the heart of the "defense decade." A big win for Chen Golan and the NextVision team for scaling Israeli defense tech to the global stage. 4. @metalbear Raises $12.5M To Power Cloud-Native Development Tel Aviv-based MetalBear, creator of the open-source tool mirrord, is helping developers run local code in production-like conditions instantly—boosting speed and reliability for teams worldwide. Already serving thousands of developers at Fortune 100 companies, the startup is scaling fast. Congratulations to @Aviramyh, @EBukchin, and the global MetalBear team for pushing cloud-native development forward. Funding was led by TLV Partners, with participation from TQ Ventures, MTF, Netz Capital, and angels including David Cramer (Sentry) and Ben Sigelman (OpenTelemetry). 5. @_RaySecurity Raises $11M To Launch Predictive Data Protection Tel Aviv-based Ray Security is pioneering AI-driven defenses that predict and protect enterprise data use in real time—claiming to reduce risk by up to 90%. Already working with Fortune 500 companies, the startup is emerging from stealth with big ambitions in cybersecurity. Great news for Ariel Zamir, Eric Wolf, Dekel Levkovich, and the Ray Security team for reshaping how enterprises secure their data. Funding was co-led by Venture Guides and Ibex Investors. For a long time, streaming architecture advice boiled down to two engines: one for high-throughput ETL, another when you need millisecond latency. At Data Engineering Open Forum 2026, Indrajit Roy (@databricks) walked through how Apache Spark Structured Streaming took a different path from day one: micro-batch processing. 🔸 Micro-batch model: Records arrive on a stream; the engine waits briefly, forms a batch, processes it, then repeats. 🔸 Batch query on stream slices: Each step is effectively running a small batch query over the latest slice of data. 🔸 One engine, different tradeoffs: The design challenges the “two streaming engines” default instead of accepting it as fixed. Watch the full keynote: https://t.co/mpQstlPVSF #ApacheSpark #StructuredStreaming #DataEngineering #OpenSource

Congratulations to the @DeepgramAI team on this integration of seamless multi-lingual support into their flagship Flux transcription model. Many voice agent use cases require that the agent understand and speak multiple languages. To support this, we often build complex pipelines with components that perform language detection and language switching. What we really want is for the models we use to just naturally understand multiple languages! Flux is fast, with integrated turn detection, and is the speech-to-text model in a very large number of production voice agents. This new multi-lingual support makes it even more useful.

What a week in Warsaw. 2,500 leaders, builders, founders, researchers, investors and public leaders came together for the @ElevenLabs Summit in Grand Theatre. There were a lot of moments that felt very special. Poland is full of energy and optimism right now. Our researchers and builders are at the frontier of AI, and the economy keeps growing at a pace that is hard not to be excited about. It was a privilege to bring so much of the ecosystem together in Warsaw - to celebrate how far we've come, and to talk about what we can build next A few personal highlights: - Welcoming the President and Deputy Prime Minister, who shared their perspectives on innovation, security and Poland’s future - with a lot of energy and optimism - Hosting former President Lech Wałęsa and showing him our original machine - assembled by @dabkowski_piotr in his Warsaw apartment at the very beginning - Previewing our newest, most expressive models yet, and live demoing the future of customer journey when booking experiences (ending with iconic voice of Piotr Fronczewski)! - Speaking with @RBrzoska about reinventing parcel delivery in Europe, and Piotr Zak about positioning an iconic Polish company, Polsat Plus Group for the decades ahead - Announcing our partnership with @LOTPLAirlines to transform customer experience with voice agents - Introducing investors from across Europe and the US to Polish founders and builders - including all our board members - thank you @slpierre, @JenniferHli, @andrew__reed - for the first time! - Having my parents come to an ElevenLabs event for the first time Thank you to everyone who came from across Poland, Europe and further afield!

More than 7 million research papers were published in 2025. That number is about to explode. Think about all of the research agents and AI writing tools that were developed last year: @OpenAI’s Prism, @elicitorg, @FutureHouseSF, @k_dense_ai, etc. What happens as adoption of those tools inevitably increases? What happens to volume when the average researcher becomes fluent in @claudeai Code? Scientific infrastructure and peer review was designed for a different world than the one we are entering. Peer review is in crisis. (Data: DOI-indexed articles on @OpenAlex_org)

Okay yeah, this is a very fun eval to test research agents on. They IMO all get it wrong over-anchoring on outdated research. ChatGPT Deep Research: 45% Claude Research: 43-47% @elicitorg's Research Agent: 49% I'd bet at least 7:1 odds on obesity <45% by 2030 (the obesity rate is currently at 37-40% depending on source). Mostly because GLP-1s likely bring it down. Claude does a good job at acknowledging GLP-1s, but... it mostly disregards them? All agents seem to fail to integrate the Gallup obesity data showing a ~3% drop in the last 3y, which is: a) lower quality, but b) more frequent, and likely consistent enough in the direction of its bias. How exactly do you solve this well? There's this tendency towards comprehensively relying on authoritative sources, but at some point you've got to reject them right? Does anyone have a LLM forecasting setup out there rn that would make better predictions for the obesity rate in 2030? (Also plausible I'm just wrong and the models are the well-calibrated ones, and GLP-1s won't change national obesity rate ig) Side-note: it's unfortunate that NHANES which published official CDC obesity data in 2y intervals was laid off, which is probably why we're missing the 2023-2025 dataset and might not end up getting it at all 💀

类似的感受，目前绝大多数和 LLM 交互的方式还是先经由人脑输出为人类的符号系统文字，再交给 LLM 处理 所以将非结构化数据转化为机器可用Token的转录工具，造就了一批爆款应用：输入法@typelessdotcom、浏览器@diabrowser、会议纪要@firefliesai、企业数据梳理@reductoai、穿戴式相机@Looki_ai 但只有转录是不够的，现实世界的语境大量存在于非文字形态，一场会议，不同人的发言顺序、话语间的停顿与犹豫、谈及一个话题的沉默与回避、语音语调和呼吸对各自陈述真实度的影响，这些是单纯的会议纪要无法捕捉的。气味声音湿度光波，每一个场景和输入形态的精确 Token 化都蕴含着很大的机会 以上还都是在技术上可被 Token 化的，只是数据量和算法的问题。但维特根斯坦意义上"不可说的"，语言边界之外的，例如审美、顿悟、直觉、默契，这些就不是数据量的问题，而是是范畴问题 就像眼睛看不见自己一样，这些就是人类创造的符号系统的边界本身，目前找不到技术上 Token 的路径，可能是AGI真的到来那天，人类真正的护城河

上一次看@FireworksAI_HQ 和curor的一个技术访谈https://t.co/Tr1jNtOHAx， 讲到curor的模型策略以及为啥自己做模型的决策： 1）通用模型的成本高于专有模型，通用模型在训练时也会用领域数据做专有训练（如coding领域），curor积累了大量的用户行为数据。 2）curor + FireworksAI 平台的策略是，自上而下（先做用户应用到基于开源基模后训练再到自己独立的预训练的完整模型建设），而基模公司是自下而上（做模型到做场景/环境应用，开放基模能力到数据反馈闭环）。curor应该走最后一步了，就是在和xai的算力合作做自己的预训练基模。 而@FireworksAI_HQ 最新的客户成功故事案例，则是和最大的法律应用@harvey 来基于开源模型训练自己的法律专属模型，核心还是成本低于通用模型而能力接近通用模型，他们发现： 1）混合法律代理可以通过选择性地将任务路由到前沿顾问，在质量和成本方面超越前沿模型。 我们测试了一种混合设置，其中 GLM 5.1 作为主工作节点，并在需要时将任务路由到 Opus 4.7 作为顾问。GLM 调用 Opus 的次数很少，平均每个任务仅调用 0.83 次。混合设置的质量和成本均优于 Opus：在相同的 100 个任务中，混合设置的平均通过率为 18%，而 Opus 为 14%；成本分别为 368 美元和 954 美元。 2 ）后训练处理可以将开源模型的性能提升到前沿法律模型的水平。 在法律代理基准测试 (LAB) 的 100 个任务切片中，SFT 将 Kimi 2.6 的平均通过率从 11% 提升到 15%，超过了 Opus 的 14%。但成本差距更加显著：在相同的 100 个任务中，混合设置的成本仅为 84 美元，而 Opus 为 954 美元，成本降低了约 11 倍。 通用模型在不断蚕食应用场景的同时，垂直的应用，在非单一模型（进行成本和能力router）+ 垂直领域数据 + 自上而下（例如curor的策略）上发力来平衡通用模型的蚕食。

With @FireworksAI_HQ we’re researching agent routing to more intelligently navigate quality-cost trade-offs for long-horizon work

Excited about this result. With @FireworksAI_HQ, we built a worker <> advisor agent. An open weight (GLM-5.1) worker delegates high-stakes / complex tasks to a closed-frontier advisor (Opus 4.7). This type of inference-time routing allows us to navigate the quality-cost Pareto frontier effectively. Lots of other cool post-training nuggets over @harvey LAB in this article, check it out!

New work with @FireworksAI_HQ on advisor models for legal agents. By leveraging open-weight models and routing to closed models for more complex tasks, we can hold frontier-level quality on LAB while reducing the cost of frontier intelligence

We partnered with @FireworksAI_HQ to train open-source models for legal. Here's what we found: 1) Hybrid legal agents can beat frontier models on quality and cost by routing selectively to a frontier advisor. We tested a hybrid setup where GLM 5.1 served as the primary worker, routing tasks to Opus 4.7 as an advisor when needed. GLM invoked Opus sparingly, just 0.83 times per task on average. The hybrid setup beat Opus on both quality and cost: 18% all-pass vs 14%, at $368 vs $954 across the same 100 tasks. 2) Post-training can push open models to frontier-level legal performance. On a 100-task slice of our Legal Agent Benchmark (LAB), SFT moved Kimi 2.6's all-pass rate from 11% to 15%, beating Opus' 14%. But the cost gap was even more striking: $84 vs $954 across the same 100 tasks, or ~11x cheaper. We're excited to continue working with @FireworksAI_HQ on the next generation of open-source legal agents.

1/5: Disclaimer: this is not a promotional post. I want to thank @floothq and founder @yyjhao from the bottom of my heart for making something which keeps our business running. Here’s how the story begins:

Most websites aren’t failing because they’re bad. They’re failing because search engines can’t see them. Today we launched Improved SEO Support on @floothq. You can now enable server-side rendering, set your primary domain, configure search metadata, and generate robots.txt + sitemap.xml directly inside Floot. Competitors like Lovable, Base44, and Replit still don’t support server-side rendering. With Floot, we’re focused on making sure the apps you build can actually be found. Comment “floot” and I’ll DM you a 30% code so you can try it out :)

“Do you use Floot yourselves?” Yes. And honestly, we couldn’t imagine running the company without it now. @floothq powers our internal systems, from admin and analytics to CRM, newsletters, and traffic tracking. Floot isn’t only for starting something new. It’s for running something real. For systems that need to evolve as your business grows, and for teams that want to stay in control of how their tools work. Using Floot internally keeps us accountable. If we can’t build and maintain our own systems with it, then we’re not doing our job. That’s the standard we’re building toward every day.

Sometimes big milestones go unnoticed. While browsing our analytics, I realized @floothq quietly crossed a meaningful one: 1,000,000+ visitors across websites built with Floot in the last 90 days. That number represents real people using real products created by our builders. One of our biggest goals with Floot has always been to make a platform where you can build something that actually works. Seeing these numbers come together feels like proof that we’re heading in the right direction. A huge thank you to every builder who’s trusted Floot to bring their ideas to life. This is starting to feel real in a new way, and it’s only up from here.

Floot just hit $1M ARR. A year ago, @floothq didn't exist. We launched just over 6 months ago with a scrappy video and zero marketing budget. I’m really proud of what @edlooky and I have built with Floot - just the two of us (thanks to my wife for catching a candid of us hard at work). It was a lot of showing up every day and figuring things out as we go. Doing this while being a full-time dad definitely made things more interesting. Time was tight, priorities were real, and balance was difficult. But it made the wins feel that much more meaningful. This milestone feels less like an endpoint and more like a good signal that we’re on the right path. There’s still a lot we want to build, and I’m excited to keep growing from here. Grateful for everyone who’s been part of the journey so far.

🚀 I’ve Been Hacking on @FlowiseAI Lately — Here’s What I’ve Built 👇 So over the last few weeks I’ve gone down a bit of a Flowise rabbit hole… in a good way 😄 I ended up opening a bunch of PRs to the project and thought I’d share what I actually did. If you haven’t used Flowise: it’s basically a super-nice visual builder for AI agents. Drag, drop, connect nodes, and boom — you’ve got a working LLM workflow. 🔧 What I’ve Been Fixing & Adding 🗑️ 1. AgentFlow list didn’t refresh after deleting Small bug, annoying experience. Fixed it so when you delete an AgentFlow, the UI instantly updates. No more manual refreshes. Tiny change, but feels way better. 🖼️ 2. Added image upload support to the ChatOpenRouter node Wanted multimodal flows inside Flowise — so now you can just drop images into the node. Opens up tons of cool use-cases. 🧾 3. Agent node can now output structured JSON Earlier, responses were mostly text. I added proper structured JSON output so people can build cleaner automations or connect flows to other tools without parsing headaches. 🚨 4. Fixed inconsistent status codes Some API calls returned weird/inconsistent errors for invalid chatflow IDs. Cleaned that up so failures are predictable and easier to handle. 🤖 5. Improved support for Hugging Face inference providers Made Flowise a bit more flexible by making model provider integrations work more smoothly with the HF inference API. 📝 6. Added image loader w/ OCR support (multiple providers!) One of my favourites — Flowise can now take in images and run OCR through different providers. Super useful for docs, receipts, screenshots, all that stuff. 🤔 Why I Did All This Honestly, I just started exploring the codebase and found small areas that could be improved. And once you make 1 PR, you kinda get hooked. Plus: Multi-modal workflows are the future JSON output makes everything easier A good user experience is basically 50% of why people love a tool Flowise is growing fast, so contributing early is fun 🎯 What I Learned Messing With Flowise OSS PRs teach you WAY more than tutorials ever will “Small things” like UI refresh bugs make a big difference Multi-modal + agent workflows get tricky behind the scenes Consistent API behavior is underrated Flowise’s architecture is actually pretty clean once you get the hang of it 🔮 What’s Next I’m planning to pick up stuff around permissions, workspaces, and maybe build some sample flows showing image-upload → OCR → agent reasoning → structured output. Also thinking of writing a guide for beginners who want to contribute. 🙌 If You’re Into AI Agents or Flowise Hit me up, or drop into the repo. There’s a lot of cool stuff happening and it’s a great project if you want to get into open-source around LLMs. Here is is list of my PRs: https://t.co/uDMqgin9uf

I want to say a big thank you to my longtime friend @javilopen, who built a fabulous AI tool called Magnific. The platform was later acquired by @freepik, and today, Freepik has rebranded into @magnific. In 2023, when Javilopen founded Magnific, I was one of his earliest users. Javilopen gifted me a lot of credits: I think 10,000 in value. I have since been using these credits, but when Freepik purchased Magnific, I started using Freepik more. I have not used the legacy Magnific platform in two years. Today, I decided to check the legacy platform and discovered my credits are intact. This is unheard of; there is no other company that would leave an unpaid subscription intact for two years and counting. I'm very grateful to Javilopen for building this awesome tool. If you aren't using Magnific, I highly recommend you check it out.

“AGI is already here, it’s just not evenly distributed yet” - @pmarca of @a16z Everyone's spending on AI. Few are getting results. We sat down with @WSJ to talk about what we see on the front lines every day at @genspark_ai: •⁠ ⁠An ad agency showing up to pitches with AI-generated storyboards ready to go •⁠ ⁠A solo consultant running 99% margins •⁠ ⁠CEOs who realized the fastest unlock is using AI themselves The companies winning aren't spending the most. They're changing how their people actually work. The gap isn't technology. It's adoption.

不會寫 Code 也能做出一個鏈上數據平台 @glassnode 24 小時自動監察系統 前一晚我臨時起意，用 Claude Code 幫自己做了一個東西，每當 @glassnode Insights 發布新的研究報告，它就自動抓取全文、用 AI 寫一份繁體中文分析、全部存進 Notion 做成一個儀表板，最後發一則 Telegram 通知提醒我 整個過程全部用白話交代 Claude 去做，最後真的設定完成，現在 24 小時自動運行中，而且也放到我們家 @monsterblockhk 的 TG 社群裡（連結放留言區！） 讓我拆解我做了什麼，大家照著做也可以！ ► 第一步：直接下指令 我在 Claude Code 裡一次把需求講清楚:要監控 Glassnode RSS、要用 Claude API 寫分析、要存到 Notion、要 Telegram 通知，並指定拆成哪幾個檔案。Claude 立刻幫我生成整個專案(十幾個 Python 檔)並自動安裝所有套件 ►第二步：接好三個帳號 Notion 建立一個 integration 取得 API key——接下來很厲害，Claude 直接用這把 key 幫我建好整個 database(欄位、分類、日期全部設定好)，我只給了一個空白 Notion 頁面連結 Telegram 到 BotFather 建立 bot 取得 token 再到 userinfobot 取得自己的 Chat ID 然後去 Anthropic 到 console 取得 API key 所有 key 我貼給 Claude 讓它自動寫入 .env ►第三步：一鍵測試 Claude 寫了一個測試腳本，一行指令就檢查四個連線Claude API、Notion、Telegram、Glassnode RSS是否暢通 老實說中間並非一帆風順，但最棒的就是遇到問題 Claude 自己除錯自己修好 比如說遇到 httpx 套件版本衝突，它立刻鎖回相容版本，或是 .env 有一個空白 key 覆蓋了真正的 key，它加上 override 解決 連 Notion 儀表板太醜，我說想做成官網那種封面圖卡片牆，它直接接上我的 Chrome 幫我把設定全部點好，做成一個 gallery 畫廊首頁 最後實測就是它真的抓到最新一篇報告全文、產出四段繁體中文分析(核心洞察、關鍵數據、市場意涵、訊號方向)、自動建立 Notion 卡片、Telegram 立刻響起。我再用 macOS launchd 設定每小時自動執行一次，之後什麼都不必做！ 你想想以後 Glassnode 一發報告，你的電腦自動幫你看完、消化完、用中文寫好分析、發到你手機，超級方便

🚨Quantum threat to #Bitcoin is becoming real! A new @Glassnode analysis estimates that nearly 10% of Bitcoin’s total supply is “structurally unsafe” in the event of a major quantum breakthrough, capable of breaking current elliptic curve cryptography. The exposed supply includes: * Early Pay-to-Public-Key (P2PK) wallets * Legacy multisig structures * Certain Taproot outputs At the same time, nearly 70% of BTC supply remains unexposed to direct quantum threats, highlighting that the ecosystem still has time to prepare, but not indefinitely. The industry conversation is evolving from theoretical risk toward practical migration paths: 🔹 Post-quantum cryptography 🔹 Quantum-safe wallet standards 🔹 Address hygiene improvements 🔹 New output formats like P2MR The broader takeaway: Quantum resilience is becoming a strategic infrastructure discussion for crypto, not just an academic one. Read more: https://t.co/RkNYIWyNWr #PQAbelian #ABEL #PostQuantum #QuantumComputing #Blockchain

Excited to expand model choice in @Glean with @NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra. The enterprise AI stack is moving toward model choice, not model monoculture. Cost-effective open models can absorb the long tail of requests and leave frontier models for the cases that actually need them. Nemotron Ultra 3 delivers 91% of frontier-model performance on key agent quality metrics and will soon be available in Glean alongside 30+ model options.

The token bill conversation is moving to the right layer. If retrieval and ranking are good, the model sees only what it needs. If not, you pay frontier-model rates to have it rediscover context on every turn. We saw this especially in building @glean voice, where there's no room for bloated context. A weaker model with a smaller, better-ranked catalog beats a stronger model working from a noisy one – on quality and on cost.

The future of enterprise AI is giving enterprises the ability to match the right model to the right kind of work. That’s why I’m excited that @NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra is coming soon to @Glean. Nemotron 3 Ultra is a strong option for everyday work. It delivers 91% of frontier-model completeness on key metrics, with the cost profile and flexibility of an open model. As AI moves from experimentation into everyday work, cost and model choice become architecture decisions, not just product decisions. This is the broader shift I’m seeing: enterprises want the freedom to use frontier models where they matter most, and open models where they can drive scale more efficiently. They don’t want to bet the future of their AI stack on a single model family. With Glean, customers can choose from 30+ open and proprietary models and route work to the best model for the job across Assistant and Agents. Thanks to the NVIDIA team for the collaboration.

Congrats to entire team at @glean for this milestone and their continued rocketship growth - surpassing $300M ARR from $100M ARR just 15 months ago! As enterprises look to adopt AI, generalized LLMs do not solve for governance, security, permissioning, and context layers - critical components for any enterprise of meaningful size. That is why the biggest companies in the world are on @glean - from eBay, Dell, Nationwide, Dominion Energy, Zillow, and more.
Great to see @glean performing well!

@glean, one of the fastest-growing enterprise AI companies, needed a support solution that could scale with them. As Kat Crichton, Manager of Technical Support, put it: "We're an AI company, so we knew we needed an AI-first solution that could move fast, was easy to implement, and would scale with us." Within three months of choosing Fin, they achieved: - 83% chat automation rate - 95% CSAT - 100% Fin involvement across all chat surfaces And the support model itself got smarter – Fin eliminated the bottleneck where everyday users had to go through administrators to get help, giving both audiences a direct path to resolution. Read the full story from Glean at the link in the replies

In 2019, the advice was "don't work on enterprise search." We disagreed: nothing at work felt as easy as Google in your personal life. So we built the index and graph. Turned out to be the grounding layer reasoning agents need now. Now, @glean just crossed $300M ARR. $100M → $300M in 15 months. Grateful to our team, our customers, and everyone who believed there was something important to build here.

I'm proud to share that @Glean has surpassed $300M ARR, just five months after crossing $200M and growing ~3x over the past 15 months. This is an exciting milestone for Glean, and it's a signal about where the enterprise AI market is heading. We’ve long believed the real challenge in enterprise AI is not access to models. It is grounding AI in how a company actually works: its people, knowledge, workflows, permissions, and systems. That’s even clearer now. The companies creating real value with AI are not just adopting better models. They are building systems that understand their business well enough to deliver reliable outcomes at scale. That is the real moat, and it is what we’ve been building at Glean: an unrivaled context layer for enterprise AI. That context has to work across the business, not just inside a single team or use case. We see that in how customers adopt Glean: more than 85% use it across five or more job functions. It also has to meet the security and governance demands of complex enterprises. We see that in who is choosing Glean: our Fortune 500 customer count nearly doubled year over year. And it has to make economic sense as usage grows. In our recent benchmark with Claude Cowork, Glean was preferred roughly 2.5x as often as off-the-shelf MCP tools and used 30% fewer tokens on average. Better context improves both quality and efficiency. I enjoyed talking with @CNBC's @dee_bosa about this broader shift. In enterprise AI, the winners will not be defined by better models alone. They will be defined by who builds the strongest foundation for enterprise context. Thank you to our customers, partners, and team for helping us build the future of enterprise AI.

LIVE at 12p PT / 3p ET: The AI bill is coming due @glean CEO @jainarvind breaks a milestone with us and tells us what CFOs are saying about tokenmaxxing @FactoryAI CEO @matanSF on model routing and cutting AI bills without cutting capability Watch here: https://t.co/LMXh06g41B

Can your AI answer these questions? - Who owns this customer escalation? - What changed on this project after last week's review? - Why did we decide to push the launch? @glean's Enterprise Graph connects people, projects, docs, and customers so AI can reason across how work actually gets done, instead of starting cold every time. More effective, less expensive. For a long time, people have asked us if they could actually see the graph. Now you can.

In a recent @Gartner note, Kevin Quinn and Radu Miclaus call @Glean “the company to beat” in enterprise context graphs. Appreciate the rigor of the analysis, as this market is moving fast. What stood out in Glean: strong enterprise adoption, deep integrations, and a trusted layer that unifies fragmented enterprise systems for AI agents. Customers don’t want impressive demos. They want AI that understands how work actually gets done, respects permissions, and drives real outcomes. Grateful for the recognition and for the customers pushing us forward.

We've talked about @Glean's Enterprise Knowledge Graph since 2019. People kept asking to see it, so we built a demo. Ask Glean something like: "show me my manager's projects and the team behind them," and watch it traverse the graph in real time, mapping work and people across the org. Very cool!

His hands rest organically between his thighs, loosely clasped at crotch level with fingers interlaced, his right thumb crossing over his left hand, gently resting his weight on his inner thighs. He sits squarely on a low, pristine white concrete wall featuring a smooth matte painted finish. Beyond him stretches a deep spatial background layered with a calm, pristine ocean reflecting small artificial lights from a distant shoreline. In the midground, a dark, thriving silhouette of a fan palm tree rises on the right, asymmetrically balancing the weathered, dark rocky silhouettes of breakwater pier structures dotted with small marker lights on the left. The scene captures the cheerful, energetic mood of a tropical vacation evening, bathed in a highly saturated split-complementary color palette. A vibrant sunset orange glows intensely at the horizon, smoothly transitioning into a deep navy midnight blue sky. The lighting is driven by a harsh, bright, direct frontal on-camera flash that freezes the subject, preserving stark specular highlights on his forehead, nose tip, cheeks, and lower lip. This intense directional hard light completely eliminates ambient fill, casting a deep black, harshly defined short shadow directly behind his right arm and torso against the clean white wall, drastically separating him from the twilight background. Captured in a travel lifestyle digital photography style with a direct-flash snapshot aesthetic, the straight-on perspective features a medium depth of field and tack-sharp focus with a touch of realistic digital noise, while post-processing heavily boosts contrast to enhance the flash-versus-night aesthetic, keeping the flash exposure neutral-warm and pushing the vibrant sunset oranges to maximum saturation against the deepened blues, framed in a 2:3 aspect ratio.

Last Friday, @smallest_AI hosted a private dinner for community and GTM leaders building in AI-native communications - with friends from @stripe, @meetgranola, @AnthropicAI, @PLAUDAI, @rootlyhq, and @SentientAGI. What stood out wasn't just the energy in the room. It was how this group of forward-thinking leaders truly showed up and opened up within such a short time frame. At every dinner we host, we curate the guest list with intention - not just who we want to meet, but who each person in the room would genuinely want to sit across from, and why it makes sense for everyone at the table to be there. In one evening, we got to know each other, exchanged notes, and explored upcoming collaborations. The closers shared what actually moves large deals forward. The conference veterans shared which ones are worth attending - and the secrets to maximizing ROI. The event hosts aligned on future collaborations. Since community and GTM are, at their core, collaborative, one of the greatest forms of leverage in this work is the network you build. And the most meaningful connections come from rooms like this - dinners structured around genuine relationships as the foundation for what comes next. Grateful for everyone who made this room what it was. 🙏
The context from meetings is worth so much - the follow up emails, the structured notes and the team update on have never been easier. I ❤️ @meetgranola ❤️ @orchid_hq

🧠 Cheap LLM does the reading. Smart LLM makes the decisions. Introducing tiny-boss — a cost-saving protocol where a free worker model (e.g. @GroqInc Llama 8B) reads your full context, and a smarter supervisor (@deepseek_ai / @OpenAI) only sees the distilled answers. Result: 81–94% savings on long-context tasks. 📄 Summarize papers ✅ 🔍 Extract entities ✅ 🪵 Analyze logs ✅ Inspired by @HazyResearch minions protocol. No local GPU needed — runs entirely on cloud APIs. 📜Link to the original paper : https://t.co/iUNNuwNNJy Made in hermes-agent @Teknium @NousResearch ⭐ Try it: https://t.co/X0yNMAbcMK pip install git+https://t.co/C72ayVg9ZV #LLM #AIEngineering #OpenSource #Python #CostOptimization #BuildInPublic

a lot of tools claim you can go from prompt to production-ready workflows.... but what does that actually look like in reality? I put @bubblelab_ai side-by-side against @gumloop in a live, no-cuts build to see how each platform handled the exact same prompt. The result: 🫧 Bubble Lab: One-shotted <2min, zero manual wiring, 3s execution 🌀 Gumloop: 25 mins setup, manual nodes, 7 min execution we believe you shouldn’t have to be a 'node expert' to automate your work

The fake lottery winner 😀 GPT Image 2 and Seedance on @Hailuo_AI Surprised ending Prompt : Create a Pixar-inspired 3D animated comedy short. A young woman receives a message saying she has won $10 million in the lottery. Shocked and excited, she tries to keep it secret, but the news spreads quickly. Friends become overly friendly, distant relatives suddenly reappear, influencers want selfies, and luxury brands begin offering her free gifts. Her ordinary life transforms into celebrity-level chaos. Believing her life has changed forever, she plans a huge celebration party. Decorations, music, food, and crowds fill the venue as everyone waits for her big announcement. The excitement reaches its peak and she prepares to reveal her fortune to the world. Just before making the announcement, she checks the message one last time and notices the fine print. The lottery notification was meant for another woman with the exact same name in a different city. Instant silence. Guests freeze. Friends disappear. Relatives leave. Brands cancel their offers. The party empties within minutes. Standing alone and embarrassed, she thinks the day couldn't get worse. Then her best friend arrives laughing and shows her another message: "APRIL FOOLS!" The entire situation was a prank that spiraled completely out of control. After a moment of disbelief, both burst into laughter. Final cinematic sunset shot of the two friends sharing pizza and laughing about the most ridiculous day of their lives. Pixar-style 3D animation, cinematic lighting, expressive facial acting, vibrant colors, emotional comedy, smooth scene transitions, dramatic build-up, humorous prank ending, movie-quality rendering, 16:9.

Prompt Drop: Alien Morning - Seedance 2.0 @Hailuo_AI Hyper-realistic cinematic sci-fi morning on an alien planet. A peaceful advanced city wakes at sunrise, filled with sleek white and silver organic buildings, curved towers, glass domes, glowing cyan details, smooth pathways, silent hover pods, red rocks, strange alien plants, and misty mountains. The inhabitants look almost human but have natural warm orange skin, subtle alien facial features, realistic expressions, and elegant everyday clothing. Show an ordinary morning routine: families leaving homes, parents going to work, children walking to school, the city slowly coming alive. 0–5 sec: wide aerial establishing shot gliding over the city as lights turn on, curved doors open, hover traffic begins, and orange-skinned families step outside. 5–10 sec: street-level tracking shot beside one family walking along a glowing curved path, parents carrying work bags, children wearing futuristic school packs, other families passing in the background. 10–15 sec: low cinematic angle near a modern alien school entrance. Parents say goodbye, children enter, workers board hover shuttles nearby. Camera rises to reveal the city fully awake. Hyper-realistic, cinematic, warm sunrise light, smooth camera movement, shallow depth of field, natural acting, detailed alien worldbuilding. Keep proportions, keep style and features, aspect ratio 16:9. Negative prompt: no text, no subtitles, no logos, no Earth buildings, no pink human skin, no cartoon style, no war, no horror, no distorted faces, no extra limbs, no chaotic crowd, no shaky camera.

I'll be honest with you. I'm an angel investor in AI startups (got @higgsfield_ai @zeely_ai @zibraai @extellaai in my portfolio among others) and I hate AI. I hate the hype, the energy consumption, the mass hallucinations being shipped as products. Hate the way it's quietly making humans worse at thinking. So why am I building an AI company with Extella? Because the disruption already happened while nobody asked us. The decision was made by a handful of labs and a lot of venture money, and now we're all living in the aftermath. You can stand outside and be right about how bad it is. Or you can get in and try to build something that makes it less bad. I chose to get in. That's what Extella is: not "AI will save us”, more like: the damage is done, let's be smart about what comes next.

They laughed when he said writing would make him rich. Then his newsletter hit 1.5 million subscribers and sold to @HubSpot for $27 million (reportedly) Sam Parr didn't go to business school rather he studied ads. Old ones,dead ones. @thesamparr hand copied them for 2 hours a day,6 months straight until the rhythm got inside him. 4 Pillar he found behind every piece of copy that converts: Pillar 1 >Architecture Structure and story. AIDA. The Hero's Journey. Frameworks that organise persuasion before a single word is written. Pillar 2 >Psychology Tension and momentum. The slippery slope. Your first sentence exists for one reason: to force the reader to the second. Pillar 3 >The Pitch Desire before price. Always. Build want so high that cost feels like a bargain, not a barrier. Pillar 4 >The Craft Simplicity and ruthless editing. Cut 50% of your draft. What's left is your actual copy. AI founders don't need more buzzwords ,they need stories that stick. Start by using Architecture to frame your product like the WSJ “two men” ad: where your user is now vs where they could be with you. Layer in Psychology with hooks that call out a specific niche.When you reach The Pitch,show how life feels after your product then casually reveal the ask (demo, waitlist, newsletter) Finally, apply The Craft: cut filler,keep verbs strong, write like you talk. Over time, these posts become a machine for recognition, subscribers, and a brand that feels bigger than your current ARR.

Evaluating agentic coding systems past vibes is brutal. Yes, we can add: Tests PM reviews PR reviews CI/CD loops But how do we know the system works reliably? While speaking with @ben_burtenshaw from @huggingface at @UphillConf, he suggested a simple but powerful idea: Model your agentic coding setup as an RL environment. Not necessarily to optimize the agents... But to measure them. And I found just the tool to help with that: → https://t.co/QRwQKYY1gE OpenEnv treats agentic execution like a Gymnasium-style environment Which means your coding agents interact with environments the same way RL agents interact with games. So you can run multiple trajectories in parallel and compute metrics instead of vibe-checking one lucky run. For example: Success rate across runs Retry frequency Tool efficiency Failure patterns Time-to-completion Cost per successful trajectory The interesting part is that rewards don’t need to be optimization signals. They can simply become observability signals for your engineering process. The architecture is also surprisingly clean And it integrates with RL tooling like: TRL torchforge SkyRL Oumi Unsloth This is an important direction for agentic engineering. Agent systems need measurable evaluation harnesses. Otherwise we’re mostly just vibe-checking outputs. Check out OpenEnv here: https://t.co/QRwQKYY1gE

The security assumption every AI team gets wrong: "As long as trust_remote_code=False is set, we are safe." ❌ We put that to the test. What we uncovered is a critical RCE vulnerability in @huggingface Transformers (CVE-2026-4372) that completely bypasses this control. A thread on how a routine model load turns into complete environment compromise 👇 1/3 🔍 The Exploit & ScaleBy abusing model configuration fields, an attacker can embed a malicious payload inside a configuration file. It executes arbitrary code even with remote code disabled. The affected versions were downloaded over 232M times while live. 2/3 🚨 The RiskSuccessful exploitation means full environment compromise—exposing cloud credentials, API keys, source code, and proprietary datasets. Impacts Transformers versions 4.56.0 through 5.2.x. 3/3 🛡️ Remediation• Upgrade to version 5.3.0 immediately. • Audit previously downloaded model configurations. • Move beyond checkbox security—static ecosystem flags aren't enough. Kudos to the Hugging Face team for the quick patch collaboration. 👇 Full technical breakdown link in the replies!

We just got recognized by Fast Company as one of the Most Innovative AI Companies of 2026!! When I joined @hume_ai right out of college, it was based on the convictions that 1. Foundational research, not just application-level software, is what drives true innovation in AI. 2. Text alone is an incomprehensive representation of the world, and therefore an incomplete foundation for training AI. 3. Voice is the most naturally adoptable modality, and within it, emotion is one of the richest signals for understanding the full nuance of human behavior. During my time here, from launching Octave 2 and open-sourcing TADA to supporting Frontier Labs and technology companies in scaling audio data, it has become apparent that the next frontier lies in the unverifiable – naturalness, expressiveness, and so on. It's an honor to be listed alongside pioneers in the field like @Google, @AnthropicAI, @theworldlabs, and @runwayml, each shaping the future of AI and its interaction with humans through distinct, earnest yet imaginative approaches. There is so much to do and very excitingly so :)

today's my last day at @hume_ai i joined three years ago as the first design hire, helping take the product from 0→1. i worked on some of the most interesting problems i've encountered in my career: how do you design for AI that listens to tone, rhythm, and emotion? how do you build interfaces for technology that doesn't have precedent? a highlight was helping shape EVI, the empathic voice interface, the first emotionally aware conversational AI. that meant figuring out how to visualize expression data in ways that felt intuitive, designing interaction patterns for a voice agent that actually responds to how you feel. those questions pushed me to think differently about what design even is when the medium keeps shifting underneath you. i'm grateful to the hume team for building that environment together. excited for what's next!

The thing that separated @hume_ai early was the founders’ focus on semantic space theory. They kept coming back to the idea that meaning lives in how something is said - pauses, stress, tone, mood etc. This is only possible if the model is trained on real conversations, not just clean, scripted audio. AKA - data is the hard part.

Life update: I’m now at @GoogleDeepMind Feels surreal to not be at @hume_ai anymore, but the team there is in an incredible position to continue advancing cutting edge voice AI research. There are changes coming in how people interact with AI. There are changes coming in how labs will teach AI to make people happy. Voice intelligence is increasingly seen as a key unlock on both fronts, a thesis Hume was very early to adopt. So Hume is sharing the infrastructure for voice data, evals, and RL to help advance voice intelligence and AI empathy across the industry. It’s proving to be a much bigger opportunity than expected. I’m excited for the Hume team and grateful to everyone who helped us get to this point. Will share more on what I’m working on soon

Am I the only one addicted to building things Today I was like okay we need an AI chat solution for @orynth First thing that came to mind was @intercom because I think it’s probably the leading chat widget for websites. But honestly it was way too much. Too many features too many options too much setup I just needed something simple. An AI chat that understands the context of my website and any extra docs I give it so it can answer users properly And if it doesn’t know the answer it should notify our human support team so we can jump in and help So I deleted Intercom Then I found @chatbase and to be fair their onboarding was really nice. For a moment I was like okay this is probably it But the feature I wanted which lets your support team jump into conversations they call it "Help Desk" and it costs $150/month And yeah maybe it makes sense if you calculate the time and opportunity cost but I was like nah I’m not paying $150/month for this So I built my own It’s now live on https://t.co/3HHZMS1khv The best part is that I connected it with a Telegram bot too because Telegram is where I am all day So now whenever a user asks for human help I get pinged directly on Telegram and I can reply from there instantly Took me a few hours to build Will probably save us $150/month for years (This is why I love building)

AI is changing the economics of enterprise software. New Futurum Research shows a growing divide between vendors that price directly on completed outcomes and those using hybrid models that combine subscriptions, consumption, and business-aligned metrics. @Zendesk, @intercom, and @DecagonAI charge for successful resolutions and completed interactions, while @Adobe, @salesforce, @ServiceNow, @UiPath, and others are linking pricing more closely to workflows executed, automation activity, and operational results. The larger market signal is that enterprise software pricing is aligning more closely with measurable business outcomes. As AI makes work outputs easier to quantify, pricing models are beginning to reflect what customers ultimately care about most: results delivered rather than licenses consumed. 📊 Explore how outcome-based and hybrid pricing models are changing the enterprise software playbook: https://t.co/USWUHi9bkX #FuturumResearch #EnterpriseSoftware #AIPricing

We're live on @ProductHunt today. Over the past two years we've helped teams like @Kajabi, @riverside, @heyjasperai, @memberstack and @pheedloop run structured A/B tests. And across 3000+ teams, we kept seeing the same problems: -No hypothesis before running a test. -Tests stopped after a few days. -"Winning" results that wouldn't survive a basic stats check. Experimentation works, but only if the process behind it is sound. So we built the course we wish most teams started with. 6 modules covering: • What to test first for maximum impact. • How to calculate sample size and test duration. • How to interpret statistical significance correctly. • How to build a repeatable experimentation system. Completely free. Tool-agnostic. If you're responsible for conversion, growth, or experimentation, this might be very valuable knowledge: https://t.co/S5vcERDekt

“I can do something now in one day that used to take two months before.” AI is forcing companies to reevaluate everything, and at our Fin x @glean x @heyjasperai meetup during @WebSummit in Lisbon, @destraynor, @jainarvind, and @timyoung explored exactly that – from how they build, sell and price products, to how they build thriving teams in the AI era. Here are some takeaways that stood out: 𝗬𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗼𝗹𝗱 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘀𝗽𝗲𝗲𝗱 𝗶𝘀 𝗻𝗼𝘄 𝗮 𝗹𝗶𝗮𝗯𝗶𝗹𝗶𝘁𝘆 As Arvind put it, our “muscle memory has been built to operate at a speed which is no longer the right speed anymore.” Work that took two months now takes a day. Teams that do not reset their pace will feel permanently behind. 𝗙𝗲𝗮𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲𝘀 𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗶𝗿𝗿𝗲𝗹𝗲𝘃𝗮𝗻𝘁 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵𝗼𝘂𝘁 𝗼𝘂𝘁𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗲𝘀 Buyers are tired of shiny demos. They want specific business problems solved, with clear KPIs, not just AI features. Timothy said it plainly, “We’re not selling you code. We’re not even selling you access, right? We’re selling you business outcomes.” 𝗘𝘅𝗲𝗰𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝘃𝗲𝗹𝗼𝗰𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹 𝗮𝗱𝘃𝗮𝗻𝘁𝗮𝗴𝗲 In a world where surface features can be replicated quickly, speed, persistence, and raw product momentum are what separate long-term winners from short-lived copycats. Take a look below for the highlights, and subscribe to the Fin Luma calendar to join future meetups in your city.

Early in our work on @JinaAI_ -ColBERT-v2 we tried pair (1) and KD (3) training ColBERT and while each step on their own improved, pair -> KD (1 -> 3) led to instability and regression in the KD step. Seems like supervised contastive finetuning is a necessary step.

Daily crypto volume reached a new all-time high on June 2. @Polymarket processed $175.8M. @Kalshi processed $107.6M. Combined, the two platforms facilitated more than $283M of crypto-related prediction market volume in a single day. The immediate reaction was to frame the event as a market-share battle. The more interesting question is why both platforms reached records simultaneously. … α/ The June 2 ATH The prediction market sector has already experienced explosive growth throughout 2026. > Prediction markets processed roughly $27B in January alone. > March volume reached approximately $25.7B across the sector. > Combined lifetime volume across Polymarket and Kalshi surpassed $150B earlier this year. Against that backdrop, June 2 produced another milestone. ➢ Polymarket crypto volume reached $175.8M. ➢ Kalshi crypto volume reached $107.6M. Both established new daily highs according to Artemis data. The key observation is that volume expanded on both venues at the same time. … β/ Two Platforms, Two User Bases Polymarket and Kalshi are often treated as direct substitutes. The data suggests they increasingly serve different audiences. Polymarket remains primarily crypto-native: > Wallet-based onboarding. > Stablecoin settlement. > Crypto-focused market participation. Kalshi’s growth has come through a different path: > Regulated access. > Traditional payment rails. > U.S.-based users. This distinction matters because growth on one platform does not necessarily require contraction on the other. The June 2 ATH demonstrates that demand expanded across both user cohorts simultaneously. … γ/ Event-Driven Trading Demand One pattern appears repeatedly throughout prediction market history. Volume spikes when uncertainty spikes. Prediction markets are not simply betting products. They are mechanisms for pricing uncertainty. Recent sector growth has been driven by: > Crypto markets > Politics > Sports > Macro events > Regulatory outcomes As volatility increases, traders seek faster ways to express views. Prediction markets increasingly function as one of those venues. The June 2 record should be viewed through that lens. Not as an isolated volume spike. But as another example of event-driven demand overwhelming prior liquidity records. … δ/ The Volume Share Question The most important metric going forward may not be total volume. It may be volume share during major volatility events. Historically, liquidity tends to concentrate during periods of heightened activity. The platforms that attract the deepest liquidity often strengthen their position after each major event cycle. For now, the data suggests neither venue is winning outright. Instead, both are benefiting from broader category growth. That is a different dynamic than most crypto exchange battles. … λ/ What June 2 Actually Proved The June 2 ATH did not prove that Polymarket is defeating Kalshi. Nor did it prove that Kalshi is taking share from Polymarket. What it demonstrated is that demand for event-driven trading continues to expand. Both platforms reached record crypto volume on the same day. That suggests the immediate story is not competition. It is category growth. And until one venue begins capturing a disproportionate share of volatility-driven flows, that remains the most defensible conclusion supported by the data.

@chrisfirst @krea_ai After Blowing all 250,000 credits basically in the pursuit of cinema and extreme accuracy and aesthetic taste to get the right images and video... I have standards. Cinematic 35mm film still, Terrence Malick style, 2.39:1 anamorphic format. Ultra-realistic close-up profile inside the cockpit of a French F8F-1B Bearcat fighter plane over Dien Bien Phu valley, Vietnam, 1954. An expressionless French pilot with sweat and red laterite dust smudges on his temple wears an Airaille Zinszner modèle 14 leather helmet, Meyrowitz 1935 goggles pushed up on his forehead, an unhooked vintage Ulmer type 12 oxygen mask, a sand-colored cotton chemisette modèle 1946 shirt with a GC 1/22 Saintonge metal pucelle insignia pinned to the pocket, an E.F.A. type 12 parachute harness with a chrome quick-release buckle, and a US surplus M7 leather shoulder holster holding a MAC 50 pistol. Outside the plexiglass canopy, two other matte, heavily oxidized sun-bleached Glossy Sea Blue (FS 15042) Bearcats with yellow-bordered Bouton d'or French roundel cocardes, four 20mm wing cannons in short aerodynamic fairings, centerline 150-gallon aluminum drop tanks, underwing twin 500-lb AN-M64 GP olive drab bombs with yellow nose bands, and 5-inch HVAR rockets fly in a tight, precise V-formation at 5,000 feet, heading 030 NNE at 200 knots. Below the aircraft, a dense, heavy white mist completely hides the deep green jungle and sharp limestone karsts under a cold, clear blue sky. Natural 3PM afternoon sun cuts through the canopy, creating warm volumetric sunbeams, subtle gold and cyan lens flares, and cinematic dust motes inside the Interior Green (FS 34151) cockpit with matte black instrument consoles, vintage analog dials, French language placard decals, and a Badin airspeed needle pointing at 200 knots. Photorealistic historical military aviation photography. This is the result. (the only complaint is the cockpit is wildly inaccurate and a frankenstein mesh of several from the French Indochina War 1951-1954). I'm still please I accomplished this. $750 of credits well spent in 24 hours.

Krea 2 Medium debuts at #6 on the Artificial Analysis Text to Image Leaderboard, trailing only models from OpenAI, Google, and NVIDIA! Krea 2 is @krea_ai's first image model family trained entirely from scratch (Krea 1 was developed in collaboration with Black Forest Labs). Krea 2 is available in two variants: Krea 2 Medium, and Krea 2 Large, which is more comparable to FLUX.2 [pro] in our arena. Notably, Krea 2 Medium outranks the larger, more expensive Krea 2 Large in our arena. Krea describes Medium as smaller and faster, with extensive post-training that makes its outputs especially stable and consistent across generations. While Large is positioned as the more capable model, our leaderboard results align with Krea's view that Medium "handles the broadest range of use cases reliably." Both models generate at 1K resolution and share a distinct set of generation controls via the API: ➤ Style transfer: Krea can extract the style of up to 10 reference images, with each image being able to be weighted in terms of importance ➤ Creativity Setting: A configurable API parameter (raw, low, medium, high) that sets how closely the model follows the prompt versus reinterpreting it ➤ Moodboards: A collection of images that can be collected in the application to apply a style transfer onto the image (separate from individual style reference images) At $30 per 1k images via Krea's API, Krea 2 Medium is priced below comparable models such as Nano Banana Pro at $134/1k images or grok-imagine-image-quality at $50/1k images. Krea 2 Large is priced at $60 per 1k images, and both models' prices increase with the use of the Style Transfer and Moodboard features. Both models are available in the Krea app, via Krea's API, and on official third-party launch partners. Congratulations to @krea_ai on the launch! See below for comparisons between Krea 2 and other leading models in our Artificial Analysis Image Arena 🧵
