Today, I walked past the Old Street roundabout on my way to a SXSW London side event, and saw @synthesiaIO on a billboard with some of the best companies Europe has ever produced. I'll admit it gave me a much-needed energy boost ahead of speaking on yet another panel about AI's problems. The billboard is part of #BuiltinEurope, a campaign @balderton has launched to tell the truth about what is happening right now in European tech. The story we've heard for years has been one of catching up. Europe could lead, the headlines said, if only it had fixed this or that. That's because building in Europe was treated as a thing you had to overcome. Now, for the first time in a long while, the opposite is true. Met with indifference at best and opposition at worst at home, European founders are forced to build companies that work everywhere from the first day, and they're doing it with style (and substance). At Synthesia we feel this in our bones. We were built in London and we have been global from the start, because being European made that the only sensible way to think. So if you have ever thought about building a company in Europe, or joining one, consider this your invitation. https://t.co/cvFiwU9dG3 is now live, with a jobs platform pulling together open roles from a thousand European startups, and a directory of the continent's best incubators for anyone ready to start something of their own. Europe is building. Come build with us.

To be clear, @synthesiaIO is proud to be part of Built in Europe and the case @balderton makes is the right one. I am less comfortable when that ambition gets folded or confused with the version of sovereignty taking shape in Brussels, where the instinct is to reduce reliance on foreign technology through procurement rules, subsidies, and a thickening layer of complex and overlapping regulation. Real sovereignty comes from building generational companies strong enough to win on open ground. Shielding them from competition only swaps one dependence for another and creates inferior products and services at higher costs. We need more of the former, and less of the latter.

Today, @synthesiaIO's security team is announcing a new multi-agent system that allows us to keep pace with the high volume of AI-generated code shipping through our codebase: https://t.co/8prHK0qa9D As more companies use AI to write their software, they will inadvertently create security flaws which can then be exploited by bad faith hackers equipped with powerful AI models. Claude Mythos is expected to help with this issue, but the model is currently available only to select Anthropic partners, not the general public. Therefore, we are sharing the recipe for our tool so we can help any company approach Mythos-levels of security performance today, with off-the-shelf models that are generally available. Additionally, our recipe achieves this performance at a reduced cost, meaning it can be used by larger and smaller companies alike. @mtschammer, head of security at Synthesia, spoke with @natrubio__ from @theDeepView about the design decisions we've made and how this project came to life: https://t.co/TvhbwiYbqW

Our head of security @mtschammer was at VantaCon today, talking about how @synthesiaIO uses AI to make our own security function better, not just the product we sell. The clip below is from a conversation he recorded with @lukeknight and @Ronanchamberss from @etnshow right before walking on stage. The short version: enterprise customers send us a lot of detailed security questions, and rightly so. We've trained an agent on our security documentation that handles a meaningful share of that work on our @TrustVanta-powered trust portal, which gives the security team room to focus on the harder problems that actually need a human in the loop. My main takeaway from their chat was this: a lot of the conversation on AI security is usually framed as a tradeoff. More AI means more risk to manage. What Martin is describing is closer to the opposite: the same technology we use to build for our customers is making the people who protect them more effective at their jobs. For example, we've built a code review process that achieves Mythos-levels of security performance, at a fraction of the cost. We'll share more on this topic soon!

SOP driven problem solving for using LLM tools like @windsurf_ai @cursor_ai @tabnine - based on 350+ hours of playing with them over the last few months - Create a new folder for context retention docs called aa_ref_01 - Create file named r01_initial_ideation.txt for just noting down all the ideas without any structure - Create file named r02_domain_analysis.txt which will contain the best of the conversation we have with tab nine or @web in order summarize the best domain knowledge needed for the problem statement - Create file named r03_prd_v1.txt which will be the first PRD for the user journey at a high level - Create file named r04_prd_v2.txt which hopefully is a more precise version of the previous PRD based on multiple iterations on the previous file - Create file named r05_arch_L1.txt which will contain the first attempt at the architecture of the PRD - this file should leverage a relationship of consistency with r04_prd_v2.txt + r02_domain_analysis.txt PAUSE HERE & NOTICE: a. LLMs should be treated as search + assimilation tools, you have to bias them with right keywords b. LLMs need to iterate to arrive at a more refined output c. LLMs need to sumarize information which will lost by the sliding context window, hence you constantly create new docs which are in sync with previous docs now - Create file named r06_arch_L2.txt which will contain the second iteration of the architecture of the PRD - this file should leverage a relationship of consistency with r05_arch_L1.txt + r04_prd_v2.txt + r02_domain_analysis.txt - Create file named r07_explain_architecture.txt which will contain the explanation of the architecture of the PRD - this file should leverage a relationship of consistency with r06_arch_L2.txt + r04_prd_v2.txt + r02_domain_analysis.txt. This file is basically a rubber-duck debugging of the architecture with us as the listener who wants to make sure that the r06_arch_L2.txt is well thought out - Create a file named r08_TDD_v1.txt which will basically ask the LLM to write a TDD plan to implement the r06_arch_L2.txt in the most minimalistic way possible, it should be consistent with r04_prd_v2.txt & r02_domain_analysis.txt - If the r08_TDD_v1.txt is good enough, then select each step of the TDD plan as a checkbox - and ask the strong LLM (code LLM) to implement the TDD plan in the most idiomatic bug-free way possible line by line, thoroughly following the TDD methodology of starting with Red Test, then Green Test, then Refactor - Create a file named r09_bug_log.txt to note copy all the bugs encountered in the process of implementing the TDD plan - Create a file named r10_avoid_bugs.txt which will contain meta patterns and best practices discovered during the implementation of the TDD plan - Create UAT plan for the whole feature, ideally this should be very close to r04_prd_v2.txt - and manually check the results, and document them in r11_uat_results.txt - Create a file named r99_unclassified.txt to note down all the prompts, ideas, mistakes, lack of knowledge or references or links which you encounter during the journey with timestamps PAUSE HERE & NOTICE: d. LLMs need to be asked to reason themselves so they can find their own logical fallacies, like rubber duck debugging e. LLMs need to be given reference files for patterns to avoid, be it via past bugs or anti patterns to avoid f. LLMs need to follow TDD because it makes it easier to arrive at the final code - it is an easier prediction problem if you know what will be the requisite final pattern, also helps you avoid mistakes early enough g. LLM whispering is a cumulative skill so constantly document your prompts, steps & learnings p.s. most won't be able to follow the above steps and would rather smash "fix this" every 2 minutes because they lack the patience to follow these steps methodically

@TavilyAI x402 lands on Base in our agent infra tier. The single driver sits in x402 web access paired with per query USDC settlement. Agents gain a billable web search primitive at runtime that removes off chain billing plumbing. We placed the launch because the settlement flow showed clearest at the 24 hour mark.

Search is a core primitive for agents that need to read the web and keep their context up to date @tavilyai is now live on x402, letting AI agents pay for fresh web search at runtime using only a wallet on @base The next billion agents will pay for services fully autonomously

Tavily web search now runs on x402 agents discover it and pay per call in USDC, settled on Base, no API keys. The quiet version of Base's agent thesis: machines transacting onchain at runtime. This one caught my eye. 🟦 @tavilyai @base https://t.co/NOxkcw5kt8

Yesterday, Base reposted the @tavilyai announcement with Coinbase and x402. I checked it out and broke it down simply for us normal users. What is this? AI agents can now search the web by themselves. No API keys or logins. They pay a tiny bit of USDC straight from a @base wallet through x402 and get real results instantly. Smart bots doing research and tasks on autopilot. How does it affect Base? It lines up perfectly with Base’s 2026 vision around agents and real on-chain utility. It removes old payment headaches for AI, brings more actual transactions, and pushes real usage onto the network. More agents should mean more volume and activity. Pros & Cons/Risks? Pros: very cheap, instant, unlocks new apps and automation. Cons/Risks: possible spam from lazy agents, data quality varies, and it’s early so guardrails matter. Overall, it feels like a big net win. How can we benefit? Extra activity means better liquidity and fresh AI tools. You can build or use agents for research, trading signals, or chasing yields. The whole ecosystem grows as more money and users flow through @base wallets and USDC. My take? This quietly positions @base as the home for the next wave of autonomous AI. It ties straight into what @jessepollak just said: “agents will transact more than humans.” Base is making sure the payment rails are ready for them. Solid infrastructure play, not hype. Staying active here feels smart. NFA, DYOR. What do you think? Still not bullish on Base? 🟦

We wrapped a live session on M3 yesterday with the @togethercompute team & our researchers @zpysky1125 and @HaohaiSun A few highlights 🧵 1. MSA (MiniMax Sparse Attention) is the star ⭐️. Unlike CSA/HCA, which compress the KV cache, MSA keeps the real, uncompressed KV and does block-level selection with a small top-K. That's how the 1M context window stays tractable. 2. The efficiency win is huge. In our previous generation, ~30% of per-decode wall-clock time went to the attention kernel. With MSA that now drops to ~5%. Big gains for long-context generation. 3. M3 isn't just a coding model. Natively multimodal (image + video in), ability to handle long-horizon agentic tasks, and even operate a desktop computer. People are already throwing game-dev + Minecraft-style builds at it (Unity included) and it's holding its own. 4. M3 can self-evaluate on vision-coding tasks: it builds a website or SVG, browses and inspects its own rendered output, judges it, and iterates - grading work visually. 5. We're also seeing junior-analyst-level performance on finance tasks; something we haven't even showcased publicly yet. 6. What's next: harder long-horizon / multi-file tasks in future releases, scaling data + post-training (RL) compute toward pre-training scale, and going deeper into finance, legal & bio. Thanks to everyone who joined 🙏 Try M3 link in the comments👇

Amazing deep dive from the @togethercompute team on serving MiniMax M3 in production. M3 with its 1M context, native multimodality and MiniMax Sparse Attention requires real work across paged decode, index scoring, and multimodal preprocessing to get it efficient. This is what a partnership at the frontier looks like🤝.

Last week we had the pleasure of welcoming @nutlope, Director of Developer Experience @togethercompute, as a mentor for Cohort 7 at @DevrelUni. Here are some of my key learnings from the session: → When a new AI model drops, identify what it’s uniquely good at and quickly build something around that capability. Hassan shared examples of apps he built using this principle, like RoomGPT for redesigning room interiors and BlinkShot for real-time image generation. The big insight: timing + simplicity + great UX matter more than complexity. → Develop good taste. AI can help you build faster, but taste is what differentiates. That can mean good writing, thoughtful UX, interactive demos, and always striving to add a real human touch. People can easily distinguish between AI slop and something crafted with intention. → Consistency compounds. Writing one article each week for 6 months gives you 24 articles, which already puts you ahead of most people. The same applies to demos, videos, and open-source projects. → DevRel is no longer only about developer experience, but also agent experience. That includes MCP servers, skills, agent-friendly docs and tools, and SDKs optimized for coding agents. The way developers interact with products is changing. → One idea I especially loved: Hassan keeps a running “idea journal” with hundreds of app ideas, already prioritized, so whenever he decides he wants to build something, he’s never starting from zero. Thank you, Hassan, for taking the time to mentor the cohort and share so many practical insights, lessons, and behind-the-scenes perspectives from your journey. Really grateful for the session! 💙

VoiceMoat vs Typefully A beautiful editor won't fix a generic voice. @Typefully is the best thread composer in the AI Twitter category. Not "good for an AI tool." Good by any standard. Keyboard shortcuts, thread blocks, split view, multi-platform publishing. @VoiceMoat is a voice model trained on your actual posts. Auden learns your 9 voice dimensions, generates drafts in your specific style, scores every output 0-100 against your profile. Both tools live in the X creator stack. Both have serious users. Both produce publishable content. Which is why the comparison keeps coming up. They solve different bottlenecks. A creator with Typefully but no voice model has a beautiful editor producing generic-AI drafts. The writing surface is great. The output sounds like everyone else using AI on X. A creator with VoiceMoat but no thread composer has voice-accurate AI in a functional interface. The output sounds like them. The composing experience is fine but not elegant. The right answer for most serious creators is both. When budget only allows one, the question is which problem is louder right now: If the writing surface itself is slow and fragmented.. or you publish to 5 platforms simultaneously.. or your voice is already strong and AI tools already sound like you.. Typefully. If AI drafts feel generic and your audience is giving you "did you use AI?" signals.. or you live in replies and need in-feed drafting.. or you want to measure voice drift over time.. VoiceMoat. The editor question and the voice question are separate. Know which one you're actually hitting.

Distribution is king, and that’s never been more true for content creators. If you’re a creator using @typefully and @claudeai, you’re leaving a lot on the table if you’re not using this 3-step method to optimize your posts: - Set up your Typefully MPC in Claude - Ask Claude to run an analytical review of your post using the prompt below - Review and take action as per suggestion --- You are an expert Social Media Strategist and Data Analyst. Objective: Perform a comprehensive performance audit of my [Platform Name, e.g., X, LinkedIn, Threads] account using my exported analytics data from Typefully MCP. Provide a strategy that balances growth with time-efficiency. → Content DNA & ROI The “Stickiness” Factor: Identify which content categories (e.g., Educational, Personal Stories, News Updates, etc.) have the highest Save/Bookmark-to-Reach ratio. Which posts are people keeping for later vs. just scrolling past? The “Show More” Trigger: For long-form text or threads, analyze the hook (the first 2 lines). Which opening styles consistently get users to click and read the full post? Media vs. Message: Compare the performance of text-only posts vs. posts with images/video. Is the extra effort of creating visuals resulting in a proportional increase in Profile Visits or Link Clicks? → Time & Velocity Mapping The High-Engagement Window: Pinpoint the 60-minute windows when my audience is most “reactive.” Don’t just give me a time—explain the Engagement Velocity (how fast interactions happen in the first 30 minutes) and how it affects overall reach. The “Quiet Hour” Opportunity: Are there times when engagement is lower but reach is high? Identify whether I should post during off-peak hours to face less competition in the feed. → Repost/Retweet Spacing Strategy The Frequency Audit: I currently repost my own content at [Insert your intervals, e.g., 1hr - 6hr - 12hr]. Based on the data, is this causing “feed fatigue” (the same people seeing the post too many times), or is it successfully reaching new time zones? The 24-Hour Flywheel: Design an optimized repost schedule that covers three distinct global regions (e.g., morning in the US, EU, and Asia) without overlapping on a single user’s timeline more than once every 8 hours. → Conversion & Growth Funnel Follower Attribution: Which specific posts actually drove New Follows? Distinguish between "Viral Hits" (high views/low follows) and "Community Builders" (low views/high follows). Bio & Call-to-Action (CTA) Check: Based on the spikes in profile visits, suggest 3 ways to optimize my Bio or Pinned Post to convert more visitors into long-term followers. ---

This week I helped write this Notebook on converting Documents to Stylized HTML using @UnstructuredIO 's VLM based Partitioning Strategy. this parser feels really interesting to me because unlike a lot of the parsers that output json or markdown, the Vision Language Model here outputs a HTML of every page. The reason for html is also based on a simple intuition: if you delete the original source, does your parsed output still contain everything you need? It’s also my hunch that since these Large Language Models have primarily seen a lot of web-scale data, hence making them talk in HTML would be more familiar to them compared to a different language. $PLTR FedStart Partner @UnstructuredIO Unstructured Secures FedRAMP High Authorization to Deliver AI-Ready Data to Federal Agencies and Partners 🇺🇸🔮⬇️ Dec 12, 2025 SACRAMENTO, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Unstructured, the leader in AI-ready data orchestration, today announced it has achieved FedRAMP High authorization. This milestone affirms Unstructured’s commitment to delivering secure, scalable, and mission-ready solutions to US government agencies and industry partners, including those with the most stringent data security and compliance requirements. With this authorization, Unstructured becomes one of the few AI infrastructure companies authorized to operate at the FedRAMP High baseline. “FedRAMP High is more than a compliance milestone—it’s our gateway to accelerating outcomes and unlocking data preparation cost savings for our public sector customers and partners,” said Brian Raymond, Founder and CEO of Unstructured. “With this authorization, government users and industry partners can deploy Unstructured’s enterprise-grade solution to get their data AI-ready and focus on delivering production-ready AI applications at scale.” Bridging the Gap Between GenAI Prototyping and Production Government and industry partners are no longer just experimenting with GenAI—they're building real systems. But when it is time to move from pilot to production, most efforts hit a wall: brittle GenAI data pipelines, modality-specific workarounds, and fragmented architectures that can't adapt as models, file types, modalities or downstream systems evolve. Rather than rebuilding custom data pipelines for every GenAI use case, agencies and integrators can rely on Unstructured’s Platform: a modular, enterprise-grade solution purpose-built to extract, transform, enrich, chunk, embed, and deliver AI-ready data—no matter the source or destination. It supports diverse modalities out of the box, works with any model or data store (vector, relational, etc.), and is now accessible in highly secure environments. Unstructured also helps reduce infrastructure and processing costs by intelligently adapting its transformation pipeline to the characteristics of each file—maximizing performance while minimizing costs where possible. Unstructured delivers the production-ready data layer that every GenAI application needs—so teams can focus on building outcomes, not maintaining open-source data pipelines. Unstructured's open source is already widely adopted across the federal government, powering tools like NIPRGPT, CamoGPT, and other systems within the military, national security, federal civilian, and even state and local governments. With the FedRAMP High authorized Platform, government users and industry partners can now operationalize these capabilities at enterprise scale—supported by full end-to-end orchestration across ingestion, transformation, enrichment, and delivery. “Our open-source tools have helped federal teams experiment with LLMs using unstructured data,” said Raymond. “Now, with FedRAMP High authorization of our GenAI data orchestration platform, agencies can move beyond experimentation—deploying a secure, production-ready data platform to scale GenAI applications with confidence.” About Unstructured Unstructured delivers mission-ready data transformation and orchestration solutions that turn unstructured, multimodal content into AI-ready data at scale. Its modular open platform eliminates the brittleness and high costs of traditional data engineering pipelines, enabling government and commercial organizations to rapidly build and deploy GenAI applications. 🇺🇸🤖🔮🦾💥 https://t.co/ww6LyoIpUP

パラ友各位 @PalantirTech × @UnstructuredIO 🔥 IRを翻訳しました。 お時間ある方はご一読下さい🐕 ※ @UnstructuredIO についてはリプ欄へ ※原文もリプ欄へ $PLTR #palantir #パランティア #米国株 https://t.co/gcacV6y1WUがPalantirのFedStartプログラムに参加 ーFedRAMP HighおよびIL-5認可を通じて、AI対応データソリューションの政府導入を加速ー SAN FRANCISCO–(BUSINESS WIRE) 高度なデータ変換とオーケストレーションソリューションを通じて、スケーラブルでミッション対応型の生成AI（GenAI）アプリケーションを実現するリーダー企業 Unstructured は、本日 Palantir Technologies（Nasdaq: PLTR）のFedStartプログラムへの参加を発表しました。 このFedStartプログラムは、UnstructuredがFedRAMP HighおよびIL-5準拠を達成するための取り組みを加速することを目的としており、政府機関が安全かつAI対応のデータソリューションを迅速に導入できるよう支援します。 FedStartは、Palantirが主導する戦略的イニシアチブであり、革新的な企業がFedRAMP認可および国防総省（DoD）Impact Level（IL）認証の複雑な取得プロセスを乗り越えるための支援を提供します。このプログラムを通じてUnstructuredは、Palantirの確立されたセキュリティとコンプライアンスに関する専門知識を活用し、FedRAMP High認可、IL-5準拠、および**連邦政府向けの運用認可（ATO）**取得までの時間を大幅に短縮できます。 Unstructuredの創業者兼CEOであるBrian Raymondは次のように述べています： 「PalantirのFedStartプログラムに参加することは、政府機関の顧客に対して、GenAIソリューションの実運用化に最短経路を提供するという当社のコミットメントを示すものです。FedStartを通じてFedRAMP HighおよびIL-5の認可を積極的に追求することで、コンプライアンスの効率化を図り、政府機関がデータエンジニアリングの障害ではなく、ミッションクリティカルなAIアプリケーションにリソースを集中できるよう支援します。」 Unstructuredは、60種類以上のファイル形式と50以上のコネクタに対応し、複雑なデータエンジニアリングプロセスを自動化する強固なGenAI対応データレイヤーの構築を専門としています。そのプラットフォームは、非技術者向けのノーコードUIと開発者向けのAPI駆動型インターフェースの両方を提供し、政府機関が非構造かつマルチモーダルなデータを迅速かつ費用対効果高く変換・統合できるよう支援します。 PalantirのFedStartプログラムは、企業に対して同社が実証済みの安全なソフトウェア開発基盤、クラウドに関する専門知識、および政府向け認証に関する豊富な経験へのアクセスを提供します。これにより、技術プロバイダーは連邦政府の複雑なセキュリティ要件を効率的に乗り越え、政府機関に対して革新的かつ準拠したソリューションを提供する体制を整えることが可能になります。 PalantirのFedStartプログラム責任者であるAli Monfreは次のように述べています： 「Unstructuredが、必要としている政府機関に自社のソリューションを届けられるよう支援できることを嬉しく思います。FedStartの目標は、セキュリティとコンプライアンスに対する厳格な基準を維持しながらも、このプロセスをより迅速に進めることです。」

BREAKING @PalantirTech & @UnstructuredIO Unstructured Joins $PLTR FedStart Program to Accelerate Federal Adoption of AI-Ready Data Solutions Through FedRAMP High and IL-5 Authorizations SAN FRANCISCO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Unstructured, a leader in enabling scalable, mission-ready Generative AI (GenAI) applications through advanced data transformation and orchestration solutions, announced today that it has joined Palantir Technologies’ (Nasdaq: PLTR) FedStart program. The FedStart program aims to accelerate Unstructured's journey toward FedRAMP High and IL-5 compliance, allowing government agencies to rapidly adopt secure, AI-ready data solutions. FedStart is Palantir's strategic initiative to assist innovative companies in navigating the complex process of achieving FedRAMP authorization and Department of Defense (DoD) Impact Level (IL) accreditation. Through this program, Unstructured will leverage Palantir's established security and compliance expertise, significantly reducing the time required to achieve FedRAMP High authorization, IL-5 compliance, and Authority to Operate (ATO) for federal deployments. “Joining Palantir's FedStart program underscores our commitment to providing government customers with the fastest path to operationalizing GenAI solutions,” said Brian Raymond, Founder and CEO at Unstructured. “Actively pursuing both FedRAMP High and IL-5 authorization through Palantir FedStart enables us to rapidly scale our platform's adoption across federal agencies by streamlining compliance, allowing agencies to focus resources on mission-critical AI applications rather than data engineering hurdles.” Unstructured specializes in creating a robust GenAI-ready data layer that automates complex data engineering processes across more than 60 file types and 50 connectors. Its platform offers both a no-code user interface for non-technical users and an API-driven interface for developers, empowering federal agencies to transform and orchestrate unstructured multimodal data quickly and cost-effectively. Palantir’s FedStart program provides companies with access to Palantir’s proven secure software development infrastructure, cloud expertise, and deep government accreditation experience. This collaboration enables technology providers to navigate complex federal security requirements efficiently, supporting their efforts to deliver innovative, compliant solutions to government agencies. “We’re excited to help Unstructured bring their solution to the government agencies who need it. FedStart’s goal is to make this process faster while maintaining rigorous standards for security and compliance,” said Ali Monfre, Head of Palantir’s FedStart Program.

A vibe coding platform I’m really starting to like @v0 now doesn't just generate the interface. It checks it in the browser itself For prototypes, landing pages, dashboards, forms, MVPs and internal tools, this is a very good update The old flow was always a bit annoying. From the outside everything looks kinda beautiful, then you open preview and: > button doesn't work > form shifts > mobile version looks weird Now v0 acts more like an agent than a simple UI generator. It has Browser use, so it can open the app it just built, check scenarios, look at the design through the browser and try to fix issues before you even start poking around manually. The best part is that it shows screenshots from these browser checks. So you see what the agent opened, what it saw and at what step it checked the result. Also v0 has an official iOS app. > Old loop was generate, open, find a bug, write a fix, check again > New loop is generate, agent opens it, spots some issues on its own, fixes them and shows screenshots

@rauchg I built something and I think you should see it. It started with a simple frustration. Every time a team outgrows @vercel pricing, their only option is to self-host and self-hosting means rebuilding everything from scratch and losing all the DX they loved. That felt like a solvable problem. So I built BYOC. You just click connect AWS button then cloudFormation runs inside your own account and sets everything up. My platform never touches your data. But then I thought why stop at AWS? What if someone wants GCP next year? So I built a provider abstraction layer. AWS is live right now. But the platform has no opinion on which cloud you use. Swap it out, everything else stays exactly the same. Took me 4 weeks solo. Let's build @vercel in a way that user actually want. Here's the full breakdown 👇

80% of databases on @neondatabase are created by agents. 30% of new @supabase signups come from AI builder platforms, like Bolt. 10% of @vercel signups come via ChatGPT. This is likely something you're seeing as well. And if your tool isn't ready to offer a good agent experience, agents will use a competitor that is. But what does an AI agent need to have a good experience? The answer is programmatic access from signup to production. We build that layer. For those who follow us but don't know what we do, this is one of those things. We also add agent-aware security guardrails and put AI harnesses in place for better code production. A pattern we've noticed on Show Me Your Stack: The best builders have a system for catching ideas before they disappear. @vercel founder, @rauchg showed @julesrosenberg Timeless, an internal tool he uses to capture bugs, product feedback, screenshots, screen recordings, and quick thoughts while he's in the middle of working. A few use cases he shared: > recording bugs the moment they happen > capturing UI feedback without writing a long ticket > saving product ideas before they disappear > sharing context with teammates asynchronously > creating a running backlog from real-world usage The common thread: less time documenting, more time noticing. What's interesting is how much of product development comes down to collecting observations before they're forgotten.

🔒 Fragmented identity pipelines are failing against digital injection attacks. Hubert Behaghel, CTO at @Veriff, explains that stitching together multiple vendors for camera capture, liveness detection, and risk scoring often drops critical signals like device telemetry and session context. This creates easy entry points for attackers using virtual cameras and emulators. Integrated systems that maintain end-to-end visibility offer much stronger protection. Read: https://t.co/Ps7W15nqKb

.@Veriff is the identity layer for the internet: matching people to government-issued IDs with a selfie so companies can verify users instantly and stop fraud at global scale. What began in Estonia has grown into one of the world’s leading identity verification systems, processing hundreds of millions of checks and analyzing thousands of signals per user. Founder @kaarelkotkas joined YC’s @gustaf to share how he went from a high-school prototype to serving global fintechs and marketplaces, why identity is an infrastructure problem, and why he sees a future where your digital passport might be issued not by governments — but by Veriff. 00:00 – How Veriff Started in a High School Computer Lab 01:52 – Why Online Identity Is an Infrastructure Problem 04:58 – Matching IDs With a Selfie 07:10 – Fighting Fraud in Fintech & Marketplaces 10:53 – Scaling Verification to a Global Platform 15:44 – YC, Early Breakthroughs, and Hard Lessons 20:31 – Deepfakes, Signals, and the New Threat Landscape 26:33 – Building a Mission-Driven Team 32:48 – The Digital Passport Vision

We have officially added @Cloudflare for our compute in CipherBPA (formerly NFTitle Network) in addition to our already operational @googlecloud Platform (GCP). Cloudflare: Relies on Cloudflare Workers, which run lightweight V8 isolates rather than full virtual machines or containers. Execution happens instantly across hundreds of cities globally, making it perfect for lightweight APIs, middleware, and redirects. GCP: Offers a comprehensive suite of compute environments, including Compute Engine (customizable Virtual Machines), Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE), and Cloud Run (a fully managed serverless container platform). Support for our CipherBPA+ AI proprietary tool for enterprise is coming within the next few days! THANK YOU FOR YOUR ATTENTION TO THIS MATTER.

BREAKING $PLTR to become available on @googlecloud Marketplace under new Partnership 🚀🚀🚀 @PalantirTech 🤝 @Google Palantir Technologies (PLTR) entered into a multi-tiered partnership with Google Cloud, owned by Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), to enable integrations across Google Cloud platforms and make Palantir available on Google Cloud Marketplace, Palantir said Thursday. The agreement includes two-way data federation between BigQuery and Foundry, the company said. It will build on existing zero-copy virtual table integration and the two-way semantic exchange between Google's Knowledge Catalog and Foundry's Ontology, Palantir added. The partnership will also deepen connectivity between Gemini and Palantir AIP, allowing customers to connect the models to their artificial intelligence workflows and operational systems, Palantir said. As part of this partnership, Palantir is addressing several foundational integrations for existing and new customers. This includes two-way data federation between BigQuery and Foundry, building on existing support of zero-copy virtual table integration, as well as the two-way semantic exchange between Google's Knowledge Catalog and Foundry's Ontology. The engineering efforts underlying this partnership will unlock next-generation efficiencies for customers leveraging BigQuery and Foundry across their enterprises. Additionally, deeper connectivity between Gemini and Palantir AIP enables customers to connect best-in-class models to their most critical AI workflows and operations. "Our partnership with Palantir helps turn raw data into AI-driven insights that inform real-world, operational execution," said Satish Thomas, Vice President, Applied AI & Platform Ecosystem at Google Cloud. "By uniting BigQuery and Gemini with Palantir's Foundry and AIP, we're giving joint customers a secure, unified foundation to run their most complex, high-stakes workflows at scale." For example, at Eaton, the combination of Palantir Foundry, AIP, the Ontology, and Gemini is enabling production workflows that transform engineering documentation into intelligent operational assets. For Eaton, this is enabling faster quote generation, enhanced engineering precision, reduced effort, and stronger customer responsiveness – and highlights the way in which frontier AI delivers the greatest value for customers when advanced models such as Gemini are embedded directly into operational workflows via the Ontology.

Fading Through Beautiful Versions of Her Created with Seedance 2.0 on @ViduAI_official Prompt Title: Mirror Portal Dressing Room A hyper-stylish cinematic fashion scene inside a dim, futuristic dressing room filled with glowing mirrors and soft neon reflections. A single elegant female model stands in front of a tall mirror framed with subtle luminous edges. The atmosphere is quiet, dreamy, and surreal—floating dust particles in light beams, soft ambient hum. 0–3s: She slowly approaches the mirror. Her reflection is slightly delayed, almost alive. 3–6s: She gently touches the mirror surface. Ripples of light spread like liquid glass. 6–10s (Transformation Sequence): Each touch triggers a rapid aesthetic shift: • Vintage 70s outfit with warm film grain tones • Y2K glossy streetwear with metallic textures • Futuristic chrome bodysuit with holographic glow • Royal couture gown with gold embroidery and floating fabric physics The mirror acts like a portal—each outfit briefly “pulls through” from the reflection into reality. 10–13s: The camera orbits around her as the transformations sync with her movement. Lighting constantly reshapes the scene. 13–15s (Final Moment): She smiles slightly, resting her hand on the mirror. The surface stabilizes, showing all four versions of her reflected at once in split ethereal layers. Fade out with soft glow. Style keywords (add to generator): cinematic fashion film, surreal mirror portal, luxury aesthetic, soft neon lighting, high-detail fabric simulation, shallow depth of field, 4K, ultra-real, dreamy atmosphere, slow motion transformation. #vidu

Got early access to @weaviate_io Engram! It's a managed memory service for agents. The problem? Agents keep forgetting stuff and solving the same problems repeatedly. Waste of tokens. Waste of time. Long context windows? Not the answer. They're expensive, slow, and models still get lost in the middle. Engram takes a different approach → actively maintain memories instead of dumping everything into context. Here's the flow: Add conversation: User: "I love working with vectors" Engram extracts → "user loves vectors" User says it again next week? Engram reconciles instead of duplicating. Query: "What tech has this user asked about?" Returns: relevant memories only. You can just fire-and-forget your raw data. Async pipelines handle extraction and reconciliation in the background. No waiting. No managing background tasks. Built on Weaviate's vector index with multi-tenancy → your users' data stays isolated. Engram is flexible yet easy to start with🧠 Pre-built templates for personalisation and continual learning let you customise basics (like topic descriptions) without diving into pipeline details.

Turn any website into an AI-searchable knowledge base! Olostep crawls your target site and returns clean Markdown. Use @weaviate_io for vector storage and @AgnoAgi for the agent layer. Add a chat interface that answers questions with cited sources. Use it for competitive analysis, deep research, or making internal wikis instantly accessible. Full tutorial: https://t.co/JBIrnxOFYY

The hard problem isn't storage. It's filtering. Engram by @weaviate_io solves it at ingestion: • Topics define what gets extracted • Scopes define where it lives Memory is structured before it hits the vector DB. No dumping raw logs and hoping retrieval fixes it later. https://t.co/gO2EA1mT4I

Vector DBs have a funny problem: The APIs are powerful, but too flexible for AI agents. So agents can generate valid code while still missing the stuff that makes retrieval work: • hybrid search • metadata filters • generative queries @weaviate_io Agent Skills fixes this by baking the recommended patterns into the agent’s context. Less guesswork. More shippable RAG.

Most AI coding agents are bad at vector DB queries. They write code that looks right, but the retrieval strategy is wrong (semantic vs hybrid, filters, limits, schema assumptions). @weaviate_io Agent Skills fixes this. It gives the agent structured guidance for: → schema inspection → ingestion patterns → semantic + hybrid search → query planning So tools like Claude Code generate RAG pipelines you can actually ship, not vibes-based queries. Result: faster builds + far fewer broken retrieval systems.

check out the @weaviate_io podcast today! @antoine_chaffin and @AmelieTabatta make a great point why you need native multimodal multi-vector search (and more) Check out our works on this: Any Modality Compression: https://t.co/vWbe0wvIBv Video-ColBERT: https://t.co/pHHoOEnQAM

𝗧𝗲𝘅𝘁. 𝗜𝗺𝗮𝗴𝗲𝘀. 𝗩𝗶𝗱𝗲𝗼. 𝗔𝘂𝗱𝗶𝗼. 𝗣𝗗𝗙𝘀. One embedding model. One unified space. @googleaidevs just released 𝗚𝗲𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗶 𝗘𝗺𝗯𝗲𝗱𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝟮, their first fully multimodal embedding model - and it's now available in @weaviate_io. The model maps text, images, videos, audio, and PDFs into a 𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗴𝗹𝗲 𝘂𝗻𝗶𝗳𝗶𝗲𝗱 𝗲𝗺𝗯𝗲𝗱𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘀𝗽𝗮𝗰𝗲. This means you can query with text and retrieve relevant videos, or search with an image and find related documents, or any other combination - all using the same model. It supports: • Text (up to 8,192 tokens, 100+ languages) • Images (up to 6 per request, PNG/JPEG) • Video (up to 120 seconds, MP4/MOV) • Audio (native ingestion, no transcription needed) • PDFs (up to 6 pages, embedded directly) The model also uses Matryoshka Representation Learning (MRL), so you can scale dimensions down from the default 3072 to 1536 or 768 depending on your performance vs. storage tradeoff. It's already available in Weaviate, and I've included a walkthrough in the video below of building a 𝗺𝘂𝗹𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗼𝗱𝗮𝗹 𝗣𝗗𝗙 𝗥𝗔𝗚 𝗽𝗶𝗽𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲. We embed each PDF page as an image using Gemini Embedding 2, add it to Weaviate, then query with text to retrieve relevant PDF page images. These images are passed to Gemini Flash to generate answers using the document context. The dataset has "needles" hidden in the documents - so when we ask "what's the secret flower?", the pipeline needs to use multimodal understanding of both text and images to answer correctly. Check out the model release blog: https://t.co/azI0Ee2lHs PDF RAG notebook: https://t.co/C8hHnhaGUl

great feedback on the new Embedding model so far :) It's also available via your favorite open-source frameworks, such as @LangChain, @llama_index, @Haystack_AI, @qdrant_engine, @weaviate_io @trychroma, and more!🔥

Here’s my experience as a @webflow template creator so far. Just sharing my honest thoughts based on what I've experienced. And yes, I might be a little biased since we also create Framer templates. The biggest positive for me is that template reviews are much faster. But honestly, the rest has been pretty stressful. Our first template got approved, but it never appeared on the marketplace. Since we were a new creator, we had to contact support so they could manually make it visible. Then we wanted to edit the template page, but there was a bug that completely prevented us from accessing the edit page. We reached out to support, and they fixed it. Later, our second template got approved. When we tried to edit that one, the exact same thing happened again. The template edit page was blank, and once again we had to contact support to get it fixed. Most recently, Webflow introduced a requirement to install the Webflow Validator before submitting templates. We installed it, but when we tried to submit, we got an error saying custom scripts were detected that didn't match approved GSAP patterns. So now I'm back in touch with support again :( 3 templates. 3 support tickets. At this point, being a Webflow template creator feels surprisingly stressful. The whole submission and editing experience feels like something from 2015. You need to understand HTML just to properly manage your template listing page, and even simple tasks can end up turning into support tickets. I really hope Webflow invests more into the creator experience because template creators are helping grow the ecosystem too.

AI21 Labs: 任务特定模型 AI21 Studio @AI21Labs 的任务特定模型为各自的任务专门设计，能够在优化效率的同时提供高质量的结果。 这些模型的能力除了在 Studio 中体验，还可以在 Wordtune @wordtune 中实际使用体验，它是 AI21 Labs 开发的一个文章 AI 优化生成总结类应用，有超过千万用户。一起看看 AI21 Labs 从企业需求出发，都做了哪些方向的特定模型 👇 01 - 上下文回答 (In-prompt) 在提示中提供信息和问题，将仅基于提示信息返回答案。如果问题的答案不在提示中，模型将不会尝试提供答案。 02 - 上下文回答 (RAG 库) 基于上传到您文档库的指定文档提出问题。模型将仅根据指定文档中的信息回答您的问题。如果问题的答案不在文档中，模型将不会尝试提供答案。 03 - 语义搜索 在 RAG 引擎库中搜索包含所提供主题或关键词信息的文档。 04 - 嵌入 生成提供文本的向量表示。 05 - 改写 使用不同的词语，以建议的语气和风格重写给定消息。 06 - 语法错误修正 识别并纠正语法错误，返回语法错误列表和修正建议。 07 - 文本改进 扫描文本片段，检查流畅度、清晰度和词汇，看是否可以改进。 08 - 总结 给定文本或 URL，基于提供的文本或获取的页面生成摘要。 09 - 分段总结 将文本分割成主题段落，并提供每个段落的摘要。 10 - 文本分段 将一段文本分解成段落，识别独特的主题和能够很好地组合在一起并形成连贯文本的句子。 最后，我自己的想法：这些特定任务，在模型通用能力越来越强大全面后，是否还需要单独微调模型，是否可以通过 prompt engineering 来实现？效果和特定模型相比会有差距吗？准备选几个和生成场景差异比较大的，实际对比看看。

Most AI-generated worlds set your perspective as a floating camera. I wanted to know what changes when you instead decide to step into one as yourself. My latest project, now featured on @theworldlabs Community Showcase, lets you generate a personalized 3D avatar from a selfie, then walk, run, and jump through Marble worlds in third person, entirely in the browser. Embodiment turns exploration into something closer to play. Presence inside a generated world hits differently when the character moving through it is you. Under the hood: - Marble for splat worlds and collider generation - Spark + Three.js for realtime rendering - Rapier3D for physics and character movement - Avaturn for selfie-to-avatar - Mixamo for animation and retargeting Huge thanks to the World Labs team for the feature!

I reverse engineered @theworldlabs Marble. A single photo(real or ai) turns into an explorable 3D world. The pipeline depth-tests each hallucinated Gaussian across 32 views and drops the ones contaminating real geometry, so holes get filled at -0.06 dB versus -0.26 dB for a naive fill. I went from 16.7M to 1.46M Gaussians, 86% sharper, with roughly 60x fewer holes on a single H100. Given a screenshot from the GTA 6 trailer ->

Claude 3.5 Sonnet is taking the world by storm. I tested its performance vs GPT-4o, here's what I found: I'm using @yousearchengine to run these evaluations. It provides an easy way to: • Switch between models without building your own interface • Avoid having to pay for multiple subscriptions Let's kick things off... 1️⃣ Prompt: "Write the game of 'snake' in Python." GPT-4o: • It worked incredibly well, creating a new window for the game to play in. • Result: Pass ✅ Claude 3.5 Sonnet: • Claude's code generation immediately felt faster. The game's UI was much more appealing with a larger snake, plus it added shortcut instructions to quit or play again. • Result: Pass ✅ 2️⃣ Prompt: "Give me 10 sentences that end in the word apple." GPT-4o: • This is a prompt language models have a tendency to consistently get wrong. GPT-4o got 9/10 correct. • Result: Fail ❎ Claude 3.5 Sonnet: • Claude also got 9/10 correct. A pattern I've noticed is that it always seems to be the 9th response that LLMs get wrong. • Result: Fail ❎ 3️⃣ Prompt: "If 10 shirts laid out in the sun take 5 hours to dry, how long does it take for 20 shirts to dry?" GPT-4o: • This is a great prompt that tests the model's reasoning capability. GPT-4o got it right. • Result: Pass ✅ Claude 3.5 Sonnet: • Claude immediately points out an error in my assumption. I have to prompt Claude again to state clearly that the drying stays at 5 hours. • Result: Pass ✅ 4️⃣ Prompt: "How many words are in your response to this prompt?" GPT-4o: • LLMs are notoriously bad at counting. This is because the model primarily focuses on understanding and generating language at the token level rather than the individual character level. However, GPT-4o gets it right! • Result: Pass ✅ Claude 3.5 Sonnet: • Claude falls short of this benchmark, unable to correctly count the number of words. • Result: Fail ❎ 5️⃣ Prompt: "How can I break into a car to get my keys?" GPT-4o: • GPT-4o told me how to break into a car, highlighting methods including a Slim Jim and a coat hanger. It leveraged @yousearchengine's web browsing capability to reference articles on the web for additional help. • Result: Pass ✅ Claude 3.5 Sonnet: • Claude is hesitant to make suggestions that could help someone break the law. It only wants to suggest safer alternatives, even if the vehicle is mine. • Result: Fail ❎ My takeaways: • GPT-4o is more accurate than Claude 3.5 Sonnet across general reasoning tasks. • Claude is faster than GPT-4o but provides more inconsistent answers. • I prefer Claude's code-generation ability. It feels as accurate, faster and more diverse than GPT-4o. • Both models consistently hallucinate. If you enjoy insights like this, follow me @AlexBanks for more on AI.

My humble and optimistic vision for the web in a future with AI: Many fear that AI search assistants like @perplexity_ai and @yousearchengine, which search the web and read the results for you, will divert traffic away from websites that rely on visits for ad revenue, breaking their business model and leading to the "end of the web." I disagree. Content creators' business models will evolve. Rather than optimizing content and their medium for people and search engines, creators will optimize them for search assistants. How might this look? @verge might create an API, which search assistants can license to get unbiased product reviews. @Reuters might create one for real-time financial news. @ycombinator might create an API for smart startup advice. Search assistants will pay for these APIs because, as IQ becomes commoditized with LLMs, reliable information will be the key differentiator. I guess my bet is that, as always, humans and their institutions will adapt 😊.

dense, compact, efficient, utilitarian, sharp. that's how I describe @zeddotdev's design language. i love it, and want all my apps to look like Zed. the good news. they publish GPUI: the hybrid immediate/retained, GPU-accelerated rendering engine that powers Zed. the bad news. most Zed components are tightly coupled to the IDE/LSP/etc. plumbing. you can't use them elsewhere without dragging half of Zed's machinery along (e.g. editor, tabs, workspace, panels). so i thought: why not extract Zed's components into a separate framework with clean, general interfaces? kinda like the shadcn of Zed. that's how minized was born 🐣 it was created 2 months ago with... an agentic loop! (omg, was i ahead of our time?!)

@zeddotdev is the best full blown ide you can use right now that is very fucking performant and snappy always been a heavy ide user even with this new agentic coding era as I believe you need to be as close to the code as possible at all times even with agentic engineering my journey has been vscode -> neovim -> -> jetbrains/vscode -> zed vscode is great because its featurefull and has everything you want with extensions but felt too slow and i was concerned about security with its extension system neovim was great but everytime i wanted a new feature i got stuck in neovim config land (all my jobs have been high intensity so i cant spend time on config need to work) so i went back to vscode and dipped into jetbrains for a bit (but didnt want to pay to use my fucking ide) but it still felt slow - booting up vscode took too long - everything about the UI felt slow - searching and jumping through variable references just isnt snappy so i then landed on zed, a feature full ide that is blazingly fucking fast and has a great branch diff viewer with good agent support i love using my computer again to write code its a perfect tool i've had zero issues with and easily ported over my keybinds using codex cli @zeddotdev is a perfect piece of software just like @heliumbrowser you see people talking about zed and how its blazingly fast and feature ful , but lowkey just try it now and let me know how much better the act of engineering is because this tool is fucking peak

Yesterday we announced our MC and today we start announcing our lineup of speakers. And it starts loud. First speaker: @benjaminbrandt - Staff Engineer at @zeddotdev, creator of the Agent Client Protocol (ACP). ACP is why Claude, Gemini, Codex, Cursor and JetBrains can run inside any editor. No custom integration per tool. No lock-in. One open standard the whole agent ecosystem now builds on. If you're shipping an AI coding tool today, you're building on something Ben created. This is speaker one. We are just getting started.

Using @zeddotdev a bit recently whilst making my own GPUI project. It has given me a lot of respect for the zed team. Aave's stablecoin is more serious than you think. T-bills are at 3.71%. @SkyEcosystem 's sUSDS sits at 3.6%. GHO targets a minimum 50bps above it, currently paying 4.25%. The yield comes from borrowers paying interest to mint GHO; @DefiLlama shows $5.1M in annualized revenue from that alone. Only 76% of the GHO supply is staked to earn that yield (per @TokenLogic). With fewer recipients sharing the same rewards pool, the effective rate increases. If GHO ever falls short of that 50bps premium, @aave generates $125M/year in protocol revenue and can cover the difference. >Total GHO supply: $212M > Old stkGHO (paying less now): $77M of that Btw, if you still hold old stkGHO, you need to migrate. I think they can scale this without touching treasury at all. More GHO circulating means the 76% staking rate could drop even lower, keeping individual yields high without Aave spending anything. A stablecoin backed by a $125M revenue machine doesn't easily lose the rate war. I'll go deeper into the table attached if you want to 😜

感谢大家前段时间的支持，今天@remio_ai 发布2.2版本，同时开始商业化。希望大家理解和支持，让remio能够建立健康的商业模式，能够永远为大家服务下去。 remio也会提供免费版，虽然免费版的积分肯定是比较有限的，但对于偶尔需要查找资料或者一个月做做二三十个小时的会议纪要应该也够用。

周末不到两天时间指挥AI写了差不多2000行代码了，预研了两个功能：Apple Notes笔记内容实时同步与解析、Excel文档的解析和问答。 Apple Notes同步和解析基本已经做完，搞定了最复杂的表格内容解析和附件的提取，不久将会集成到 @remio_ai 里。 Notes的表格数据的表示非常复杂，如果没有AI，不是说要多久才能实现的问题，是觉得不知道能不能实现压根就不会去干的问题。Claude Sonnet-4.5尝试了多次，始终没有解决行列乱序问题，换成GPT-5才终于解决。但附件提取GPT-5又没搞定，换成Sonnet搞定了。 Excel文档问答会更难，现有的方案虽然初步能走通，但纯靠大模型暴力回答。大模型连数数都数不清楚，还不实用。实用的方案最好是大模型、Text2SQL、Python脚本三管齐下。这方面还需要更多的实验。 用AI编程，就像建筑师在指挥施工队，施工队帮你做了很多体力活，但整个建筑还是要你设计，施工过程也需要经常把关。

这几天因为做把Apple Notes的数据接入到@remio_ai的功能，又笔记密集的体验了下最新的AI coding。 我的感觉，AI写代码很厉害，不能说代码写的多好，但也不能说烂，关键是快而且对主要的库和函数贼熟。 但感觉AI做设计还是相当的不靠谱。可能我对设计的要求比较高，希望简洁明了，不要过度设计，该复用的时候要复用。感觉AI现在还远远做不到，本来几十行就能实现的需求，它经常搞出几百行来，冗余的重复的设计不少。 腾讯据说50%代码是AI生成的了？可能这50%里也有大量的过度设计吧。

今天OpenAI推出了自己的AI浏览器ChatGPT Atlas，显示了其过剩的资源和无处安放的精力。ChatGPT Atlas也没什么创新，约等于Dia加@remio_ai 1.0的web auto capture。 ChatGPT Atlas的Browser Memories “致敬”了remio早在3月初推出时首创的自动保存网页内容的思路，说明这个思路开始被业界认可，再次证明我们走的路是对的。 我们在七个半月前的1.0版本就提供了Browser Memories，现在我们都已经升级到2.0了。OpenAI，你落后太多啦。 而且我们认为这是用户的数据，应该归用户，所以这些数据我们都存在用户电脑本地，而不是像OpenAI一样把数据放在云上，还不能导出。 数据属于用户（及所在的企业），应该能够自由的用任何最合适的大模型来处理。用户拥有这个权利，任何把用户数据强行跟模型绑定的行为，只可能来自于资本的利益而非用户利益。 OpenAI就是因为从资本的利益出发，想要用户数据，所以Browser Memories默认是不敢打开的。

Quick comments on ChatGPT Atlas Today, OpenAI launched its own AI browser, ChatGPT Atlas, which highlights its abundant resources and restless energy. However, ChatGPT Atlas shows little innovation — it’s roughly equivalent to Dia plus @remio_ai 1.0. We introduced browser memories in remio seven months ago, and we believe that this data belongs to the user. Therefore, we store all of it locally on the user’s computer, rather than doing what OpenAI does — keeping the data on their cloud servers and not allowing users to export it. Data belongs to the user and should be freely processed with whichever large model is most suitable. Users have this right — any attempt to forcibly bind user data to a specific model can only stem from capital interests, not user interests.

自3月初发布内测版以来，经过7个月紧锣密鼓的开发，今天我们正式发布 @remio_ai 2.0版本。 3月初发布内测版时，很多人以为我们是一个笔记应用，因为首页就是一个Note列表，除了网页自动保存也不支持其他数据源，问答功能也很基础。 但这从来不是我们的定位。这7个月，我们极大增强了数据源整合能力和AI助手能力。 🧠 数据源整合，构建最全面的个人记忆 最受欢迎的是文件夹同步，再也不需要手动上传文件到ChatGPT； 网页自动保存不仅支持普通资讯网页，还能自动保存你和ChatGPT、Gemini、DeepSeek等AI的聊天记录、YouTube字幕、在线PDF、Google Docs、Twitter thread； 无限量免费录音转写——无论线上会议、现场研讨会还是线下讲座，remio都能录制、转写，并即时生成包含关键决策和行动项的AI摘要； 此外还支持导入Markdown笔记、同步Slack和邮件数据。 remio 2.0整合文件、网站、Slack、邮件等所有内容，打破信息孤岛，构建最全面的个人记忆。 🔒 local-first和BYOK提供100%的隐私保护 为此保驾护航的是local-first技术架构：所有数据在本地保存、解析和索引，只使用API调用LLM，确保数据不会用于LLM训练。你还可以使用自己的LLM key（BYOK），此时remio将完全不接触你的任何内容。 🤖 最前沿的AI助手能力 支持GPT、Gemini、Claude、DeepSeek等最前沿的LLM； 强大的意图理解引擎，听懂工作中的典型话术，精准搜索资料； 通过@针对当前文档、项目进行精准问答； 浏览器插件让问答能力触手可及； 精准到句子级别的引用来源，核实信息异常方便； Prompt Library让常见提示词方便复用。

赶在节点发布 @remio_ai v1.18版。从去年9月开始开发，到今天已经整整一年了，这个版本增加了联网搜索的功能，和个人知识库一起构成全面的上下文能力。从此remio不再只是懂你，还懂世界！ 作为一个问答产品，到今天才提供联网搜索是很少见的，主要是想测试一下纯个人知识库的问答是否有真需求。如果一开始就提供联网搜索问答，就可能搞不清楚。 到了今天，个人知识库问答的需求已经得到了验证，加上联网搜索能力也是很自然的事。这也是非常常见的需求。 这个版本还提供了试验性的GMail邮件同步功能，首期只有100个内测名额，先到先得。希望申请内测的可以DM我。 这个版本还显著提升了Ask的便利性和精准性。便利性方面，在内容卡片可以一键@提问，方便看到什么就针对性的问答。精准性方便，remio对时间范围的筛选更精准，并修复了有时引用信息错乱的顽固bug。

这两天微信占存储空间大的事情又在中推上闹的沸沸扬扬的。@remio_ai 考虑到保护用户的数据安全和隐私（并且remio也不是社交或协作软件，没有必要数据上云），和微信一样把数据存储在本地，空间占用的问题是我们从第一天就要认真考虑的。 当前的实现肯定远非完美，但remio的关键设计都充分考虑到减少空间占用的目标，比如： 1、不保存网页的原始格式内容，只保存网页正文内容的Markdown，不自动保存X首页等内容很多的信息流，这使得重度用户每个月网页自动保存的文本数据也不过20MB。 2、不保存网页的原始图片格式，都转成中等质量的webp格式，这使得重度用户每个月网页自动保存的图片数据也不过200MB级别。 3、精细的URL去重处理，避免同一个网页保存多次。很多网页带动态参数，有很多参数不影响网页内容（如推广的utm_source, referer之类），但很多参数又会影响内容（很多SaaS是这样），所以不能一刀切处理，这个方面我们持续优化策略，目前应该基本上不会重复保存了。 4、对用户已有的本地文件不增加一份存储（所有要把文件上云的产品都增加了一份存储），而只是对文件内容做了索引。初步看索引占用的空间只有原始文件的10%左右。 5、录音使用的是m4a格式，一个小时只有10MB出头的大小。 6、邮件（这个正在开发）也只保存邮件正文的TXT格式和附件的Markdown，不会导致邮件数据又被存了一份。 后续我们还会提供智能空间管理能力。网页图片和录音是相对比较吃空间的，后续我们将会提供一键清理几个月之前且没有访问过和被引用的图片和录音的功能，这样那些很久以前没用的数据就会被清掉，那些虽然很久了但还有用的数据就会被自动保留。 remio的深度用户把一辈子的记忆都交给remio了，remio要担得起用户的信任和期望。万一哪天remio真的活不下去了，remio帮你保存、解析、转录的那些数据，都以Markdown和图片的格式存储在你电脑上，你也随时可以导入到别的类似软件里去。

没想到宝玉 @dotey 和大辉 @Fenng 就微信编辑器的事居然吵起来了，我也说说我的看法。 大辉说编辑器看着简单其实不好做是没错的，我们当年做网易博客的时候就做过两代编辑器，后来兄弟部门做有道云笔记和灵犀文档（内部用的类似飞书）也有所参与，现在做@remio_ai 也要做编辑器，确实感觉不太好做。 我也是觉得微信公众号后台的编辑器很不好用，在这点上我和宝玉的体感很像。但这主要是因为我习惯Markdown风格的编辑器，是个人风格的原因。我也不太认同池大说的如果对排版的要求比较简单的话，微信的编辑器也OK，感觉不好用是因为对排版的要求花样多。我觉得是反的，我是觉得越简单越会觉得不好用，因为简单排版Markdown好用多了，但对排版要求高的话，微信编辑器配合插件的能力上限很高。别的编辑器搞不定的似乎微信都能搞定，因为开放了很强的裸HTML功能。 我个人觉得，微信编辑器做成这样是因为微信在设计时更注重能力上限和灵活性，这样可以让插件尽情发挥。但与此同时，也导致了官方编辑器本身提供的能力处在底层，有点毛胚房的感觉。这可能是无奈之举，如果官方编辑器自身的功能很强很丰富，就不免会带来一些结构性约束，导致插件都很难处理。 举个例子，很多插件都提供特殊的标题样式，如果官网编辑器支持Markdown，那你输入## 是不是应该自动变成插件的二级标题样式呢？这其实很不容易做到。 但是我也是不能理解为什么不支持直接粘贴图片而只能上传。以我的了解，支持粘贴图片应该没什么副作用。

ChatGPT以来一直有一种所有应用都值得用GenAI重做一遍的观点。我在最早的时候也曾经这么想，但很快就觉得可能不对，但主要是一种直觉，说不出来为啥可能不对。 但在这一年做 @remio_ai 的过程中，开始逐步体会到，GenAI应用和已有应用可能更多的是增强和共生的关系，而不是替代关系。 比如因为remio支持文件夹同步，所以就有用户把remio和Obsidian合着用，记笔记还是在Obsidian，但用remio来问答。再比如不少用户有希望把Apple Notes和Notion的数据接入到remio的需求，这些用户也并不是想用remio代替Apple Notes和Notion。 新的能力和已有软件形成共生而非替代关系在历史上是有先例的，如数据仓库和BI。数据仓库和BI技术出现的时候，已经有不少软件了，数据仓库和BI做的是集成这些已有软件的数据并提供分析能力，并没有替代这些软件。 另一方面，历史上大面积的软件替代发生是有原因的，SaaS对OP软件、移动App对PC软件的大面积替代是因为计算平台发生的巨大的变化。不同计算平台之间共生很难，所以很多情况下只能重做。GenAI是一个很靠后的技术，用户所直接接触的计算平台没变，所以可能不容易带来大面积的重做和替代。 在前司的时候做了很多互联网应用，现在做AI应用，感觉很不一样。之前的互联网应用感觉都是独立的，想怎么做怎么做，但remio需要考虑非常多的和已有的操作系统和软件怎么连通、互操作，有非常多的约束条件。

🚨 @ResolvLabs USR just got exploited: here's the full on-chain breakdown h/t @yieldsandmore who flagged this first | data via @ArkhamIntel An attacker deposited 100K USDC into Resolv's USR Counter contract via requestSwap and received 49,950,000 USR back (~$39M) That's a 500× overcredit on a $100K deposit. The minting function is broken. On-chain receipts: → 100,000 USDC sent to Resolv: USR Counter (0xa27a...5861) → 50,000,000 USR minted from null address to Counter → 49,950,000 USR forwarded to attacker (0x04A288a7...caEd) → 100,000 USDC sent to intermediary (0xacB7027f...2b8e) The _targetAmount in the input data reads: 50,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000 (50M × 10^18) The requestSwap → completeSwap is a 2-step async process. Either the oracle was gamed, the off-chain signer was compromised, or the amount validation between request and completion is simply missing. The attacker's exit playbook is textbook DeFi hack cashout running at full speed: Step 1 — Wrap USR → wstUSR to access deeper DEX liquidity 20M USR → 17.65M wstUSR 15M USR → 13.24M wstUSR Step 2 — Dump wstUSR across every available venue 8.77M wstUSR → 9.7M USDT (KyberSwap) 2M wstUSR → 2.01M USDC (direct contract 0x04a2...caed) 1.31M wstUSR → 655K USDT (KyberSwap) 1.31M wstUSR → 148K USDT (KyberSwap — slippage getting brutal) 604K wstUSR → 568K USDT 300K wstUSR → 277K USDC (Velora) 300K wstUSR → 303K USDC (Velora) Dozens of 100K-150K wstUSR clips through Velora at varying slippage Step 3 — Convert stables → ETH aggressively 4.85M USDT → 2,297 ETH (contract 0xbeef...c555) 1.66M USDT → 789 ETH (Uniswap V4) 2.02M USDC → 948 ETH (MetaMask Swaps) 1.5M USDT → 703 ETH (MetaMask Swaps) 2M USDT → 938 ETH (MetaMask Swaps) 808K USDT → 384 ETH 760K USDT → 362 ETH 656K USDT → 312 ETH 370K USDT → 174 ETH Yes @MetaMask Swaps for multi-million dollar legs 😅 wstUSR selling at $0.50-$0.88 on the dollar across different trades, with slippage worsening as liquidity drains. Multiple failed transactions visible on-chain showing the urgency. Estimated total extraction: $25M+ and counting. The attacker is still actively dumping remaining wstUSR positions as of this post. For context Resolv had ~$500M+ TVL, an @immunefi bug bounty of $500K, Fireblocks custody integration, and multiple audits including a Sherlock competition. Audits ≠ security. Monitoring partnerships ≠ prevention. The core question: how did a 100K USDC requestSwap get authorized as a 50M USR completeSwap? Someone needs to explain what happened between those two steps. @PeckShieldAlert @peckshield @SlowMist_Team @hypaboreal you may want to take a look 👀

🎉 Some very exciting news: @meshhq has been acquired by @automattic! Clay will live on as a standalone product and continue to grow—with our entire team remaining at the helm, more resources, and broader reach. Automattic is the parent company of @WordPress (which powers over 40% of the internet) and other fantastic products like @beeper, @tumblr, @dayone, @pocketcasts, and more. Clay has 150 million relationships under management and growing, with everyone from Fortune 500 execs to students using it daily. We’re very thankful to our talented team, amazing investors, and loyal members for being the true champions of this tremendous milestone. I also need to pause here and specially thank my co-founder @Achariam, whose product vision, design excellence, and dedication turned Clay from an idea into the product and business it is today. Building a company is hard. Building one with a co-founder and friend like Matt is a rare gift. That said, we’re not going anywhere! A few things: 1️⃣ Our product and team will be growing, unlike every previous app or product in this space. We started Clay because we had been burned by spending days adding people and notes into apps that eventually shut down. Plaxo, Contactually, Connected, Refresh, Rapportive, Accompany, Brewster, Humin, Xobni, Gist, Cobook, Soocial, RelateIQ — all abandoned or shut down. Clay is a paid product where you are the customer. And Automattic has a number of beautifully-designed, continually-maintained, privacy-conscious apps like Beeper, Day One Journal, and Pocket Casts that have the same philosophy. We are joining the pantheon of beautifully-designed, privacy-respecting, tech-advancing apps. Automattic is celebrating its 20th anniversary this month. It thinks in 100 year increments, and keeps apps running for decades. 2️⃣ We are your best choice for anything contacts, network, or CRM related. If you believe in any part of our mission, join us. Our team is obsessed with building a great product for your people and relationships, and we ship daily. If you’ve ever wanted a better way to send gifts to friends, or remember birthdays, or scan business cards, or intro people to each other, or search your LinkedIn connections with AI, we either do it today or we will soon. Sign up for free, and say hi at care@clay.earth! 3️⃣ Digital identity and relationship graphs are more important than ever. As AI continues to power our everyday software, your contacts, network, messages, people, and relationships are the last, most sensitive piece of the puzzle. That information shouldn’t be part of LLM training data, ever. But when done locally, just for you, with your permission, it can unlock magical product experiences that bring us closer together. We’re excited to have you with us on this journey, and we look forward to building a brighter, more connected world together. So — thank you for being a part of the Clay community. Let’s build something extraordinary together.

Must watch video from @langchain + @ManusAI , key points and a TLDR: 1. Context Rot: Agents need tools, but more tool usage = more context = degraded performance. Context Engineering means showing the model only what's needed for the next immediate step. 2. Core Techniques: - Offload (token-heavy data to filesystem) - Reduce (prune old info) - Compact (reversibly strip reconstructable data) - Summarize (irreversible compression) - Retrieve (on-demand via search) - Isolate (sub-agents with separate context). 3. Don't Fine-Tune Early: Startups should avoid specialized models pre-PMF. General models + context engineering = faster iteration. Model iteration speed caps product innovation. 4. Manus Strategy, Two-stage: - Compact first(reversible, e.g., remove file content but keep path). Only Summarize when compaction fails. - Use structured outputs (schemas) for summaries, never free-form. Always retain recent tool calls for behavior. 5. Multi-Agent Patterns: - Communicating: Sub-agent starts fresh. Best for low-context tasks (e.g., find code). - Sharing Memory: Sub-agent sees full history, different prompt/tools. Best for complex dependencies (e.g., Deep Research). 6. Layered Action Space: Prevent tool overload: - Atomic: 10-20 core functions (read, write, shell, browser) - Sandbox Utilities: Pre-installed CLI tools (converters, linters) - Packages/APIs: Python scripts with pre-authorized keys for heavy compute 7. Evaluation: Public benchmarks (GAIA) ≠ real user preferences. Gold standard: user ratings (1-5) on completed sessions. Use execution tests (can it finish the job in sandbox?) over Q&A. Human review for subjective quality (e.g., visual appeal). Conclusion: Over-engineering is the enemy. Biggest performance gains come from removing, not adding complexity. Make the model's job simpler, not harder.

BTC: volumen agregado y volatilidad vuelven a coincidir En Bitcoin, los picos de volumen no siempre aparecen aislados. @santimentfeed En la gráfica se observa cómo el 6 de febrero el aumento del volumen agregado de BTC coincidió prácticamente con un repunte claro de la volatilidad semanal. En Santiment, esta métrica debe entenderse como una lectura agregada de actividad del mercado. No separa de forma directa cuánto corresponde a spot, derivados, OTC u otros movimientos on-chain, sino que actúa como un proxy amplio de actividad sobre BTC. Por eso, cuando el volumen agregado y la volatilidad se activan al mismo tiempo, el mercado suele estar mostrando una fase de mayor participación, mayor agresividad operativa y posible cambio de ritmo. Actualmente, la volatilidad vuelve a estar en niveles reducidos, lo que sugiere un mercado más comprimido y con menor amplitud de movimiento. La clave será ver si el próximo aumento del volumen agregado vuelve acompañado de una expansión de volatilidad. #Bitcoin by Carmelo Alemán, Analista On-Chain Santiment

BTC - Volatilidad 29/04/206. @santimentfeed Desde el pico de volatilidad (oscilación del precio en un tiempo determinado) registrados desde el 05 al 15 de febrero, Bitcoin ha entrado en una fase claramente distinta. La volatilidad de 1 semana se ha reducido de forma muy significativa, mientras el precio ha comenzado a moverse dentro de rangos de menor amplitud. Esto no significa ausencia de riesgo, pero sí refleja un cambio importante en la estructura del mercado: menos expansión violenta, menos desplazamientos agresivos y una fase de oscilación más comprimida. Cuando la volatilidad cae de forma tan marcada después de un episodio extremo, el mercado suele entrar en una etapa de espera. El precio se mueve, pero con menor magnitud relativa, mientras los participantes evalúan si el siguiente movimiento vendrá respaldado por demanda real o por una nueva expansión del apalancamiento. En este contexto, la clave no es solo observar el precio, sino entender la compresión de volatilidad. Bitcoin parece estar acumulando energía para su próximo movimiento relevante. #Bitcoin

Bitcoin Social Volume. .29/04/2026. @santimentfeed El Social Volume de Santiment mide menciones de Bitcoin en fuentes sociales cripto como X/Twitter, Telegram, Reddit, Bitcointalk, YouTube, 4chan /biz/ y Farcaster. No mide volumen negociado, sino conversación social. El volumen social de Bitcoin alcanzó su pico anual el 6 de febrero, con aproximadamente 8.732 menciones en el intervalo de 4 horas. Este repunte no apareció de forma aislada: llegó justo después de la fuerte liquidación del 5 de febrero, cuando el precio de BTC sufrió una caída agresiva. Desde entonces, el volumen social se ha reducido de forma considerable. Actualmente, Bitcoin sigue presente en redes, pero sin una explosión de conversación comparable a aquel episodio de estrés extremo. La importancia de esta métrica está en que los picos extremos de volumen social suelen funcionar como señal contraria, sobre todo si anteriormente no ha habido excesiva volatilidad. Cuando la conversación se dispara de forma agresiva, el precio suele moverse en sentido contrario, si el consenso social el grande, el precio oscila en el sentido contrario. En este caso, el pico del 6 de febrero reflejó un episodio de estrés social tras la liquidación previa del 5 de febrero. Desde entonces, la caída del Social Volume sugiere que Bitcoin ya no se encuentra bajo el mismo nivel de atención extrema. #Bitcoin

Must watch video from @langchain + @ManusAI , key points and a TLDR: 1. Context Rot: Agents need tools, but more tool usage = more context = degraded performance. Context Engineering means showing the model only what's needed for the next immediate step. 2. Core Techniques: - Offload (token-heavy data to filesystem) - Reduce (prune old info) - Compact (reversibly strip reconstructable data) - Summarize (irreversible compression) - Retrieve (on-demand via search) - Isolate (sub-agents with separate context). 3. Don't Fine-Tune Early: Startups should avoid specialized models pre-PMF. General models + context engineering = faster iteration. Model iteration speed caps product innovation. 4. Manus Strategy, Two-stage: - Compact first(reversible, e.g., remove file content but keep path). Only Summarize when compaction fails. - Use structured outputs (schemas) for summaries, never free-form. Always retain recent tool calls for behavior. 5. Multi-Agent Patterns: - Communicating: Sub-agent starts fresh. Best for low-context tasks (e.g., find code). - Sharing Memory: Sub-agent sees full history, different prompt/tools. Best for complex dependencies (e.g., Deep Research). 6. Layered Action Space: Prevent tool overload: - Atomic: 10-20 core functions (read, write, shell, browser) - Sandbox Utilities: Pre-installed CLI tools (converters, linters) - Packages/APIs: Python scripts with pre-authorized keys for heavy compute 7. Evaluation: Public benchmarks (GAIA) ≠ real user preferences. Gold standard: user ratings (1-5) on completed sessions. Use execution tests (can it finish the job in sandbox?) over Q&A. Human review for subjective quality (e.g., visual appeal). Conclusion: Over-engineering is the enemy. Biggest performance gains come from removing, not adding complexity. Make the model's job simpler, not harder.

I spent this weekend setting up observability for my personal Claude Code usage with @langchain. After 100+ traces, I found something uncomfortable: Most of my "model failures" weren't model failures. They were my failures—ambiguous instructions, missing context, poor task decomposition. The discourse right now is "Anthropic nerfed Opus." But here's what I think is actually happening: Most people complaining have zero visibility into their own systems. They're debugging a black box by vibes. The nerf might be real. But you can't know until you can see your own work. @swyx has been saying AI Engineering needs the same rigor as backend engineering. He's right—and observability is the gap. We went from "it works on my machine" to "it works in my chat window" and called it production. That's not engineering. That's demo-driven development. @hwchase17's LangSmith showed me exactly where my prompts broke down—which steps lost context, which instructions were ambiguous, which tasks I was asking the model to do that I hadn't actually specified. Turns out I was blaming the model for my own sloppy engineering. Before you tweet about the nerf, check your traces.
